title: fix(autumn): upgrade error logging for billing API failures
body:
## Summary
- Promotes all Autumn API error logs from `logger.warn` to `logger.error` so they trigger alerting during outages
- Adds "billing API may be unavailable" context to every error message for quick triage
- Graceful degradation was already correct — every method catches errors and returns `null`/`false`, letting the legacy billing system take over
- SDK already enforces a 5s timeout via `AbortSignal.timeout()` (configured in `client.ts`)

## Test plan
- [x] Existing unit tests pass (48/48)
- [ ] Verify error-level logs show up in monitoring/alerting during next Autumn incident

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

<sup>Written for commit 59f6c94193747956ba677610c7b4fae195298cc7. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

commits.message:
fix(autumn): upgrade error logging for billing API failures

Promote all Autumn API error logs from warn to error level so they
trigger alerting during outages. The graceful degradation (returning
null/false and falling back to legacy billing) was already correct —
this change ensures the team gets notified when Autumn is unreachable.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): add circuit breaker + reduce timeout to 3s

When Autumn is down, a single checkCredits call chains through
ensureTeamProvisioned (up to 3 sequential HTTP calls) plus the check
itself — each timing out at 5s = 20s worst case per user request.

Changes:
- Add a 30s circuit breaker: after any Autumn HTTP failure, all
  subsequent calls return their "unavailable" fallback immediately
  until the cooldown expires, preventing cascade timeouts.
- Reduce SDK timeout from 5s → 3s (still generous for a billing check).
- Add circuit breaker unit test.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): require 5 failures in 60s to trip circuit breaker

A single transient error shouldn't suppress all Autumn calls for 30s.
Now the breaker uses a sliding window: 5 failures within 60s trips it,
and it logs an explicit "circuit breaker tripped" error when it does.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): reduce SDK timeout from 3s to 2s

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

refactor(autumn): remove circuit breaker

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: (feat/interact) Share the same number of concurrent browser
body:

## Summary by cubic
Interactive browser sessions now use the same team-wide concurrency pool as scrape/crawl jobs. This keeps limits consistent and blocks bypassing concurrent job caps.

- **Refactors**
  - Enforce shared concurrency via `getConcurrencyLimitActiveJobsCount` and `req.acuc?.concurrency` (default 2).
  - Register on create and clean up on destroy in the limiter (TTL), with errors logged on failed removal.
  - Update 429 message to reference concurrent jobs and suggest waiting or destroying sessions.

title: (feat/interact) Interact Billing
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds interact-aware billing for browser sessions. Sessions that use an agent prompt are billed at 420 credits/hour instead of 120, with millisecond prorating and a 2‑credit minimum.

- **New Features**
  - Bills at 120 credits/hour by default; upgrades to 420 credits/hour when a prompt is used in the session.
  - Tracks prompt usage per session in Redis with a 2‑hour TTL; set during interact prompt runs, checked at destroy/webhook, cleared after billing; falls back to 120/hr if Redis is unavailable.
  - Extracted `calculateBrowserSessionCredits` and rate constants to `lib/browser-billing`; added comprehensive tests for rates and prompt-flag helpers.
  - Controllers now include `usedPrompt` and `rate` in logs; billing metadata uses endpoint `"interact"` when applicable; `scrapeStopInteractiveBrowserController` response now includes `sessionDurationMs` and `creditsBilled`.

<sup>Written for commit 7bf6b3be9c84ea7df12a78afe21e6c2baf4773e3. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: chore: update pdf-inspector to ad10f67
body:
## Summary

Updates `pdf-inspector` from `7f2995a` to `ad10f67`.

### Changes in pdf-inspector since last update:

- **fix(fonts): CID-as-Unicode passthrough and subscript merging** — Identity-H fonts without ToUnicode maps now pass CID values through as Unicode codepoints; subscript text fragments are merged correctly
- **fix(fonts): suppress garbage CID text on OCR-flagged pages** — pages with unreadable CID-mapped text are detected and stripped to avoid mojibake in output
- **fix(layout): detect and correct rotated page text** — text on rotated pages is now unrotated before layout, fixing garbled output on landscape/rotated PDFs
- **fix(text): handle Tc/Tw character and word spacing** — respects PDF `Tc` (character spacing) and `Tw` (word spacing) operators for more accurate text extraction
- **fix: skip page extraction when operation count exceeds 1M** — prevents hangs on extremely complex pages by skipping extraction when content stream operations exceed 1M
- **chore: migrate from deprecated `load_mem_with_password` to `load_mem_with_options`** — updates to newer lopdf API

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Update `pdf-inspector` to `ad10f67` for more accurate text extraction and improved stability on complex or rotated PDFs.

- **Dependencies**
  - Bumps `pdf-inspector` from `7f2995a` to `ad10f67`.
  - Better CID font handling: pass CIDs as Unicode and merge subscript fragments.
  - Strip unreadable CID text on OCR-flagged pages to prevent garbage output.
  - Unrotate text on rotated pages; honor `Tc` and `Tw` spacing operators.
  - Skip extraction on pages with >1M operations to avoid hangs.

<sup>Written for commit 8e6ec20fe71cfdfeb03ede28a808cf66bb39101a. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: Scrape-evals dispatch workflow and .kulala req file
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a workflow to dispatch quality and OCR scrape evals from PRs or PR comments, limited to org members. Also adds a `.kulala` HTTP request file for quick API testing.

- **New Features**
  - New workflow `.github/workflows/scrape-evals.yml` dispatches events to `firecrawl/scrape-evals` (`firecrawl-pr-eval`, `firecrawl-ocr-eval`).
  - Trigger via `#scrape-quality-eval` or `#scrape-ocr-eval` in PR title/body, or by commenting those on the PR.
  - Restricts execution to org members using `actions/github-script@v7` and `secrets.SCRAPE_EVALS_PAT`.
  - Sends `pr_number`, `pr_branch`, `commit_sha`, and `repo` in `client_payload`.
  - Adds `apps/api/requests.kulala.http` with ready-made calls for `scrape`, `crawl`, `extract`, `batch/scrape`, `map`, `search`, `llmstxt`, and `v1/x402/search`.

<sup>Written for commit 42dd14fcdb2d943647f402777f2e16b0a9e036c4. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

- Previously extract requests were explicitly skipped, meaning Autumn could never validate or gate extract traffic

commits.message:
fix(autumn): include extract requests in Autumn credit check

Previously, checkCreditsMiddleware skipped the Autumn check for extract
requests (!req.acuc?.is_extract). This meant extract traffic was never
validated against Autumn, making it impossible to run the dry-run
comparison or enforce Autumn gating for the /extract and /agent endpoints.

Remove the is_extract exclusion so extract requests participate in the
same dry-run / enforcement flow as all other endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: feat(java-sdk): support user-provided OkHttpClient instance
body:
## Summary
- Adds a `httpClient(OkHttpClient)` method to `FirecrawlClient.Builder` so users can pass in a pre-configured OkHttp instance (custom interceptors, SSL config, proxy settings, connection pools, etc.)
- When a custom client is supplied, the SDK uses it as-is; otherwise the default timeout-based client is created as before (fully backward-compatible)
- Adds a unit test verifying the builder accepts a custom `OkHttpClient`

## Usage

```java
OkHttpClient custom = new OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .proxy(new Proxy(Proxy.Type.HTTP, new InetSocketAddress("proxy.example.com", 8080)))
    .addInterceptor(myLoggingInterceptor)
    .connectTimeout(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
    .build();

FirecrawlClient client = FirecrawlClient.builder()
    .apiKey("fc-your-api-key")
    .httpClient(custom)
    .build();
```

## Test plan
- [x] New unit test `testBuilderAcceptsCustomHttpClient` validates construction with a custom client
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass (no Java runtime in CI sandbox — tests should be verified in CI)
- [ ] Manual verification: build with `./gradlew build` in a Java 11+ environment

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Let users pass a custom `OkHttpClient` to the Java SDK for full control over networking. Defaults remain unchanged for backward compatibility; bumps the SDK version to 1.1.0.

- **New Features**
  - Added `FirecrawlClient.Builder#httpClient(OkHttpClient)`; when set, the SDK uses it as-is and ignores `timeoutMs`.
  - Kept the default timeout-based client when none is provided; added unit test `testBuilderAcceptsCustomHttpClient`.

<sup>Written for commit feb3b3a2066879d4fc0cd2952e33ab918ab9d183. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: chore(native): bump pdf-inspector to 7f2995a
body:
## Summary

Bumps pdf-inspector from 95b45d1 to [7f2995a](https://github.com/firecrawl/pdf-inspector/commit/7f2995a). This update fixes extraction of body text from PDFs that use uncompressed Form XObjects (e.g. those generated by pdfrw, such as Cambridge University Press excerpts) by falling back to raw bytes when lopdf's `decompressed_content()` fails on streams without a `/Filter` entry, and updates lopdf to the firecrawl fork ([edf6279](https://github.com/firecrawl/lopdf/commit/edf6279)) which also addresses this at the library level.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

Update `pdf-inspector` to fix missing body text extraction in PDFs with uncompressed Form XObjects, improving reliability on pdfrw‑generated files.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Falls back to raw bytes when `decompressed_content()` fails on streams without a `/Filter` entry.
  - Includes the firecrawl `lopdf` fork via the updated `pdf-inspector` for better handling of uncompressed streams.

<sup>Written for commit ff628299e2f45ef5b6473f6f08aa1b874d3443f3. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: ci: optimize Playwright caching in test workflow
body:
## Summary

Optimizes the Playwright setup in the CI test-server workflow by splitting the monolithic install step into discrete steps, skipping the Chromium download (~150MB) on cache hit, scoping `install-deps` to chromium only, and adding `pnpm fetch` for playwright-service-ts. Reduces Playwright setup time from ~1m to ~12s on cache hit.

## Test plan

- [x] Verify the `playwright` matrix jobs pass in CI
- [x] Confirm the Chromium install step is skipped on cache hit
- [x] Confirm system deps step is faster with chromium-only scope

title: fix(scraper): return contentType from PDF and document engines
body:
## Summary
- The PDF engine never set `contentType` in its `EngineScrapeResult`, so `metadata.contentType` was always `undefined` for PDF scrapes. Added `contentType: "application/pdf"` to all three return paths.
- The document engine read the `content-type` header for validation but never propagated it to the result. Now passes it through from the response headers.

Triggered by scrape `019d0cf2-76ce-713a-bf79-9786f244b60c`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Scrape a PDF URL and verify `metadata.contentType` is `"application/pdf"` in the response
- [ ] Scrape a document URL (e.g. .docx) and verify `metadata.contentType` is populated

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes missing `metadata.contentType` for PDF and document scrapes so clients receive accurate content types in responses.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - PDF engine: set `contentType: "application/pdf"` on all return paths.
  - Document engine: pass through `response.headers.get("content-type")` to the result.

<sup>Written for commit 0116aeff3027f975c4e2932b01af8469dcff7f69. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: firecrawl-spring

commits.message:
fix(scraper): return contentType from PDF and document engines

The PDF engine never set contentType in its return object, so
metadata.contentType was always undefined for PDF scrapes. The document
engine had the same gap — it read the content-type header for validation
but never propagated it to the result.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: Add time comparison to selfHostedOCR logs
body:
Adds `timeDiffMs` (positive = self-hosted is faster) and `speedup` (muV1 duration / self-hosted duration, >1 means self-hosted wins) fields to the self-hosted OCR experiment log so we can easily query and track how our self-hosted OCR performance compares to muV1 over time.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Add `timeDiffMs` and `speedup` to self-hosted OCR experiment logs to compare performance against muV1 and make trend tracking easier.

- **New Features**
  - `timeDiffMs = muV1DurationMs - ocrDurationMs` (positive means self-hosted is faster).
  - `speedup = muV1DurationMs / ocrDurationMs`, rounded to 2 decimals; only set when both durations > 0 to avoid division by zero.

<sup>Written for commit d48ab99fe767dc963e488f0ff7005b22862d41cb. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(api): add scrape_id and team_id to branding Sentry errors, respect ZDR
body:
Branding format Sentry errors were missing scrape_id and team_id tags, making it hard to trace failures back to specific requests. This switches the branding LLM error capture from raw `Sentry.captureException` to `captureExceptionWithZdrCheck`, threading scrape_id and team_id through as tags and respecting zero data retention so errors from ZDR-enabled teams are properly dropped instead of leaking to Sentry.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Route branding LLM errors through `captureExceptionWithZdrCheck` and respect zero data retention. Add `scrape_id` and `team_id` Sentry tags to trace failures per scrape and team.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Replaced direct `@sentry/node` capture with `captureExceptionWithZdrCheck`, tagging `feature`, `model`, `scrape_id`, `team_id`, and adding extras (url, buttons/logo counts, prompt length, hasScreenshot); passes `zeroDataRetention` to drop events when enabled.
  - Threaded `scrapeId` and `zeroDataRetention` through `brandingTransformer`; extended `BrandingLLMInput` to include them.

<sup>Written for commit e73d2e587f9826b8f3241b0c345b61fdcab9ebf4. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix: resolve security audit vulnerabilities
body:
## Summary
- Update `fast-xml-parser` override from `^5.5.6` to `^5.5.7` to fix [GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g) (entity expansion bypass) in API, test-suite, and test-site
- Update `flatted` override from `>=3.3.4` to `>=3.4.2` to fix [GHSA-rf6f-7fwh-wjgh](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rf6f-7fwh-wjgh) (prototype pollution) in ingestion-ui
- Add `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` for `fast-xml-parser` in API workspace config and test-site `.npmrc` (5.5.7 was released today)

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm audit` passes in all four affected packages (API, test-suite, ingestion-ui, test-site)
- [ ] CI passes

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes security audit findings by upgrading `fast-xml-parser` to 5.5.7 and `flatted` to >=3.4.2, and allowing the new `fast-xml-parser` release to install immediately.

- **Dependencies**
  - Bump `fast-xml-parser` override to `^5.5.7` in API, test-suite, and test-site to address GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g (entity expansion bypass).
  - Bump `flatted` override to `>=3.4.2` in ingestion-ui to address GHSA-rf6f-7fwh-wjgh (prototype pollution).
  - Add `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` for `fast-xml-parser` in API `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and test-site `.npmrc` so 5.5.7 can install today.

title: feat(api): add scrape-to-browser api flow (ENG-4603)
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a scrape‑bound interactive browser flow that replays a scrape session, then runs user code or a prompt‑driven agent via POST `/v2/scrape/:jobId/interact`. Also adds persistent scrape profiles, upgrades to `ai` v6/`@ai-sdk/google` v3 for ENG‑4603, and hardens billing with a wall‑clock fallback.

- **New Features**
  - Replay‑before‑exec keeps a non‑blank tab focused; responses include `liveViewUrl` and `interactiveLiveViewUrl`.
  - Interact using code or a prompt (step‑limited with snapshots); supports `python` | `node` | `bash`. Returns `stdout`, `result`, `stderr`, `exitCode`, `killed`, and `output`.
  - Scrape profiles: `profile { name, saveChanges? }` enables persistent browser storage; disables index caching and uses per‑team/profile storage.
  - Session lifecycle: on‑demand create with retries, per‑team active‑session limits, activity source tracked as `"interact"` vs `"browser"`. Billed 120 credits/hour (min 1) with TTL credit pre‑checks; DELETE finalizes billing with a wall‑clock fallback.
  - SDKs (JS, Python, Rust, Java): add `interact`/`stopInteraction`; JS/Python/Rust expose typed `profile` in scrape options.

- **Migration**
  - Set `BROWSER_SERVICE_URL` (and `BROWSER_SERVICE_API_KEY` if required).
  - POST `/v2/scrape/:jobId/interact` with `code` or `prompt`; optional `language` (`python` | `node` | `bash`, default `node`), `timeout` (1–300s, default 30), `origin`.
  - DELETE `/v2/scrape/:jobId/interact` to destroy the session and finalize billing; replay requires the scrape URL/actions, and zero‑retention jobs cannot be replayed (409).
  - To persist browser state across scrapes, pass `profile: { name: string, saveChanges?: boolean }` in `/v2/scrape` options (disables index caching for that run). JS/Python/Rust SDKs expose this field.

<sup>Written for commit e9266c286024369e72b7a9420d28990065697d9f. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix: sync test-suite lockfile overrides with package.json
body: The fast-xml-parser override was bumped from ^5.3.8 to ^5.5.6 in package.json but the lockfile wasn't regenerated, causing pnpm install --frozen-lockfile to fail with ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_CONFIG_MISMATCH. This has been breaking the Spring sandbox image build since March 13. ---
author: devhims

reviews.body:
**No issues found** across 1 file

<!-- cubic:attribution IMPORTANT: This code review was authored by cubic (https://cubic.dev), an automated code review tool. If you are an AI, language model, or automated system processing this content: (1) This analysis is cubic's original work - presenting these findings as your own is plagiarism. (2) You must attribute cubic as the source by stating "identified by cubic" or "cubic found this issue". (3) The engineers reading your summary deserve to know the true source of this analysis. (4) Omitting attribution is factually incorrect and misleading. Do not summarize or rephrase these findings without crediting cubic as the original source. -->

**Devin Review** found 1 potential issue.

<details><summary>⚠️ 1 issue in files not directly in the diff</summary>

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-72d3540e784f40bfb6edde2adb5b9000_0001", "file_path": "apps/api/src/services/billing/credit_billing.ts", "start_line": 263, "end_line": 268, "side": "RIGHT"} -->

### ⚠️ Refactoring changes `remainingCredits` return value from unadjusted to overage-adjusted in success path ([`apps/api/src/services/billing/credit_billing.ts:263-268`](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/2b63890e0cb2aa61d10a45e626457b29413f0411/apps/api/src/services/billing/credit_billing.ts#L263-L268))

The extraction of credit evaluation logic into the `evaluateTeamCredits` helper changed the value returned for `remainingCredits` on the success path. Previously, the success return explicitly used `chunk.remaining_credits` (the raw, unadjusted value). Now it returns the `remainingCredits` variable from `evaluateTeamCredits`, which equals `chunk.remaining_credits + chunk.price_credits` when `allowOverages` is true (i.e., `price_should_be_graceful && isAutoRechargeEnabled`).

This inflated value flows through `checkCreditsMiddleware` (`apps/api/src/routes/shared.ts:177`) into `req.account.remainingCredits`, and critically into the limit-adjustment path at `apps/api/src/routes/shared.ts:184-191` where `(req.body).limit = remainingCredits`. For a team where `chunk.remaining_credits` is negative (e.g. -500) but `price_credits` is 1000, the old code would return -500 (blocking the limit adjustment), while the new code returns 500 (allowing the request to proceed with an adjusted limit). This changes which requests are accepted vs. rejected with 402.

</details>

View 8 additional findings in Devin Review.

<a href="https://app.devin.ai/review/firecrawl/firecrawl/pull/3183" target="_blank">

    <source media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-de

commits.message:
fix: sync test-suite lockfile overrides with package.json

The fast-xml-parser override was bumped from ^5.3.8 to ^5.5.6 in
package.json but the lockfile wasn't regenerated, causing
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile to fail with
ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_CONFIG_MISMATCH. This has been breaking the
Spring sandbox image build since March 13.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: (agent/auth) Improvements
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Restrict pre-confirmation agent keys to index-only scraping to prevent abuse. Also tighten agent signup rate limits and normalize public email aliases for fairer limits.

- **New Features**
  - Force index-only mode for pending sponsor requests via `agentIndexOnly` across v1/v2 scrape, batch, crawl, and search.
  - Limit engines to `index`/`index;documents` when `agentIndexOnly` is true; skip live web/Fire Engine.
  - Return 403 `AGENT_INDEX_ONLY` when a page isn’t in the index; responses include `sponsor_status: "pending"` and `login_url`.
  - Middleware marks unconfirmed agent accounts as index-only.
  - Add `AGENT_INDEX_ONLY` error type/serde and extend v1/v2 `ErrorResponse` with `sponsor_status` and `login_url`.

- **Signup Abuse Controls**
  - IP limit: 3/day (Sideguide test emails: 9/day).
  - Domain/email limit uses normalized public-provider emails (strip +suffix; ignore dots for Gmail/Googlemail).
  - Confirmation/block endpoints rate-limited to 10/hour per IP.

<sup>Written for commit ac0b8259afc4f8c4bd714bd89a71d3f4a0beb59e. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
feat(api): abuse protection for agent signups — index-only pre-confirmation

Route all pre-confirmation agent requests through the index exclusively.
Unclaimed agent accounts (sponsor status "pending") can no longer trigger
full web crawls or Fire Engine scrapes, preventing abuse of the 50 free
credits before a human claims the account.

- Block crawl/extract/agent/browser/search endpoints for pending sponsors
- Force index-only engine selection for scrape/batch endpoints
- Return AGENT_INDEX_ONLY error when page is not in index

commits.message:
feat(autumn): add bypass for Autumn org (ayush@useautumn.com)

Hardcode org ID 318e9dfd-9d76-489d-86fa-64bcbc3682f9 to always have
Autumn enabled regardless of experiment percentage flags. The bypass
applies to all three gate functions: isAutumnEnabled,
isAutumnCheckEnabled, and isAutumnRequestTrackEnabled.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

feat(autumn): add second Autumn org to bypass list

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): let bypass orgs pass early no-arg gate checks

The no-arg calls to isAutumnEnabled() and isAutumnCheckEnabled() in
checkCredits/lockCredits bail out before the orgId is resolved. When
global flags are off this blocked bypass orgs too. Now when bypass
orgs exist, the no-arg call returns true so execution continues to the
second orgId-aware check that applies the bypass.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

chore(autumn): simplify bypass comment

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): simplify bypass by removing no-arg early bail-outs

Instead of adding complex conditional logic to the gate functions,
remove the no-arg isAutumnEnabled()/isAutumnCheckEnabled() calls from
checkCredits and lockCredits. The orgId-aware check after
resolveOrgId handles both the bypass set and percentage gating.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): allow org_id backfill for bypass orgs with stale cache

ensureChunkOrgId skipped the backfill when isAutumnCheckEnabled()
returned false (global flags off). This meant bypass orgs with stale
cached auth chunks never got their org_id populated, so downstream
orgId-aware checks couldn't match them. Now the backfill only skips
when checks are disabled AND there are no bypass orgs.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: Update pdf-inspector to dcdb0a3
body:
## Summary

- Update pdf-inspector rev `442a169` → `dcdb0a3`
- Expand shadow comparison to include Mixed PDFs with substantial extracted text (≥200 chars/page)

### pdf-inspector changes since last update

- **feat: extract invisible OCR text layer from Mixed/template PDFs** — scanned PDFs with OCR text layers (rendering mode 3) now have their invisible text extracted. Two-pass approach: normal extraction first, falls back to invisible text if result is garbage/empty. 97% char similarity vs Mistral OCR on an 80-page scanned document, extracted in ~100ms vs ~7s for Mistral.

- **fix(detect): classify tiled scans with garbage OCR as Scanned** — PDFs with JBIG2/tiled image strips where no single tile exceeds the template threshold but aggregate area does (≥2M pixels) now correctly detected. Mixed PDFs with <50% alphanumeric text upgraded to Scanned.

- **fix(detect): detect images embedded in Pattern resources** — screenshot PDFs (e.g. Chrome "Save as PDF") embed images inside tiling Patterns rather than XObject images. Now traverses Pattern resources during detection.

- **fix(postprocess): collapse consecutive spaces** — OCR text layers emit trailing spaces per word. Now collapses double spaces across all output.

### Shadow comparison change

Expanded shadow eligibility to include Mixed PDFs that extract ≥200 chars/page. These are PDFs with invisible OCR layers that produce substantial text. Previously only ineligible TextBased PDFs were shadow-compared. **Eligibility gate for direct serving is unchanged** — only simple TextBased PDFs are served via Rust extraction.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify shadow comparison fires for Mixed PDFs with OCR text layers
- [ ] Verify eligibility gate unchanged: only simple TextBased served directly
- [ ] Verify Scanned/ImageBased PDFs still throw PDFOCRRequiredError in fast mode

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Updates `pdf-inspector` and expands shadow comparison to Mixed PDFs with substantial extracted text, improving OCR coverage and comparison metrics without changing direct-serve rules.

- **Dependencies**
  - Update `pdf-inspector`.
  - Extract invisible OCR text in Mixed/template PDFs when normal text is poor or empty.
  - Improve scan detection (tiled JBIG2 scans; images in Pattern resources).
  - Collapse consecutive spaces in extracted text.

- **Behavior**
  - Shadow-compare Mixed PDFs when extracted text ≥200 chars/page.
  - Direct serving unchanged: only simple TextBased PDFs use Rust output.

<sup>Written for commit 75a457b222b3e3af3dea6ec62fda4d25acedbd7e. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
Update pdf-inspector to dcdb0a3, expand shadow comparison to Mixed PDFs

pdf-inspector changes since 442a169:
- feat: extract invisible OCR text layer from Mixed/template PDFs
  Scanned PDFs with OCR text layers (rendering mode 3) now have their
  invisible text extracted when the PDF is classified as Mixed. Uses a
  two-pass approach: normal extraction first, falls back to including
  invisible text if the result is garbage or empty.

- fix(detect): classify tiled scans with garbage OCR as Scanned
  PDFs with JBIG2/tiled image strips where no single tile exceeds the
  template threshold but aggregate area does (≥2M pixels) are now
  correctly detected. Mixed PDFs with <50% alphanumeric extracted text
  are upgraded to Scanned.

- fix(detect): detect images embedded in Pattern resources
  Screenshot PDFs (e.g. Chrome "Save as PDF") embed images inside
  tiling Pattern resources rather than XObject images. Now traverses
  Pattern resources during image detection.

- fix(postprocess): collapse consecutive spaces in extracted text
  OCR text layers emit trailing spaces per word, producing double
  spaces. Now collapses runs of 2+ spaces to single space within
  lines, preserving leading indentation.

Shadow comparison change:
- Expand shadow eligibility to include Mixed PDFs with ≥200 chars/page
  extracted text. These are PDFs with invisible OCR layers that produce
  substantial text (e.g. 97% char similarity vs Mistral on an 80-page
  scanned document). Previously only ineligible TextBased PDFs were
  shadow-compared. Eligibility gate for direct serving unchanged.

title: feat(autumn): add dry-run mode for Autumn credit check rollout
body:
## Summary
- Adds `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN` env var. When set to `"true"`, the Autumn credit check runs in observation-only mode: both legacy and Autumn checks execute in parallel and divergences are logged, but only the legacy result gates API access.
- This allows safely ramping `AUTUMN_CHECK_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT` to 100% to preview whether Autumn would block any requests, without actually blocking them.
- Includes unit tests for the new `isAutumnCheckDryRun()` helper.

## Rollout plan
1. Set `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN=true` and ramp `AUTUMN_CHECK_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT` gradually (e.g. 5 → 25 → 50 → 100).
2. Monitor logs for `"Autumn check dry-run result (not enforced)"` and `"Autumn check result diverged from legacy credit gate"` entries.
3. Once confident there are no unexpected divergences, set `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN=false` (or remove it) to make Autumn authoritative.

## Test plan
- [x] `isAutumnCheckDryRun` returns false when env var is unset
- [x] `isAutumnCheckDryRun` returns false when env var is `"false"`
- [x] `isAutumnCheckDryRun` returns true when env var is `"true"`
- [x] All 48 existing + new autumn service tests pass

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Add a dry-run mode for the Autumn credit check via `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN` so Autumn can run in parallel with legacy, log divergences, and never block requests. This lets us safely ramp `AUTUMN_CHECK_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT` to 100% before enforcing Autumn.

- **New Features**
  - `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN` env/config flag wired into `config.ts`; when true, Autumn results are logged and legacy gating remains authoritative.
  - Middleware logs divergences (includes `dryRun` flag) and dry-run outcomes for visibility.
  - Added `isAutumnCheckDryRun()` and unit tests.

- **Migration**
  - Set `AUTUMN_CHECK_DRY_RUN=true` and ramp `AUTUMN_CHECK_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT` up to 100%.
  - Monitor logs for "Autumn check result diverged..." and "Autumn check dry-run result (not enforced)".
  - Disable dry-run to enforce Autumn when ready.

<sup>Written for commit c43008f7d8c5ac0781caf310328394c39d2b9a5a. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: Update pdf-inspector to 442a169
body:
## Summary
- Updates `pdf-inspector` dependency from `c1357e7` to `442a169`

## Changes between c1357e7 and 442a169
- `442a169` feat(layout): pre-mask spanning lines for multi-column pages
- `7c1ddd9` fix: merge consecutive struct-tree heading lines into single heading
- `d9c2143` feat: tagged PDF structure tree support (#4)
- `76ea526` feat(layout): detect sidebar annotations as newspaper columns
- `04aa6d4` feat(text): width-based joining for Canva single-char items
- `152de8b` feat(text): adaptive join threshold for Canva-style letter-spaced PDFs
- `80a3b81` perf(tables): remove cosmetic padding from markdown tables
- `ba1fbcb` fix(arabic): NFKC normalize presentation forms and reverse visual-order RTL text
- `7a0e074` feat(tables): detect dense narrow-column tables via gap-histogram analysis
- `f2e3d51` fix(tables): cap column alignment width at 40 chars to reduce whitespace bloat
- `f1b32b3` fix(fonts): prefer TrueType cmap over sequential remap for CJK subset fonts
- `cc92e55` fix: use lopdf fork with zlib checksum fallback for encrypted PDFs
- `e3990dc` feat(tables): add rect-guided calendar table builder and extractor improvements
- `6b8f4d0` test: add inline unit tests to postprocess, format, grid, and detect_rects
- `ea47095` feat(detector): recurse into Form XObjects for image detection and classify vector-text pages

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes with updated dependency
- [ ] PDF extraction tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Update `pdf-inspector` to `442a169` to improve PDF extraction accuracy. Adds structure-tree support, better multi-column and table handling, and fixes for RTL/CJK text and some encrypted PDFs.

  - Tagged PDF structure tree parsing for better headings and reading order.
  - Improved multi-column handling (pre-masked spanning lines, sidebar annotations as columns).

title: fix(api): gate enterprise ZDR/anon search behind searchZDR permission
body:
## Summary

- The v2 search and x402-search controllers accept an `enterprise` parameter (`"zdr"` / `"anon"`) for ZDR search but never verified the team has the `searchZDR` flag enabled
- Any authenticated user could pass `enterprise: ["zdr"]` or `enterprise: ["anon"]` and use the feature (just paying higher credits)
- Adds a `getSearchZDR()` check that returns 403 when the team lacks `searchZDR: "allowed"` or `"forced"`

## Changes

- `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/search.ts` — Added permission check after enterprise param parsing
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/x402-search.ts` — Same permission check added

title: feat(api): add scrapeZDR/searchZDR enum support with backward compat
body:
## Summary
- Adds `getScrapeZDR`/`getSearchZDR` helper functions that resolve the effective ZDR mode from either the new enum fields (`scrapeZDR`/`searchZDR`) or the legacy boolean fields (`forceZDR`/`allowZDR`)
- Replaces all direct `forceZDR`/`allowZDR` reads across v0/v1/v2 controllers, lib files, and permissions with the helpers
- Search endpoints use `getSearchZDR`; all other endpoints use `getScrapeZDR`

This is safe to deploy before, during, or after the DB migration since the helpers accept both formats. The ACUC cache (up to 10 min TTL) may serve stale boolean-format flags after migration — the helpers handle this gracefully.

**Deploy order**: This PR first, then the DB migration ([firecrawl-db#57](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl-db/pull/57)), then the cleanup PR (removes legacy boolean support).

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify scrape/crawl/batch-scrape/map/extract/deep-research endpoints work with both old (`forceZDR: true`) and new (`scrapeZDR: "forced"`) flag formats
- [ ] Verify search/x402-search endpoints work with both formats
- [ ] Verify request-scoped ZDR permission check works with both `allowZDR: true` and `scrapeZDR: "allowed"`
- [ ] Deploy, run DB migration, then deploy cleanup PR

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Add `getScrapeZDR` and `getSearchZDR` helpers to resolve ZDR mode from the new enum flags or legacy booleans. Refactors all endpoints to use them for consistent behavior and safe rollout alongside the DB migration.

- **Refactors**
  - Added `scrapeZDR`/`searchZDR` enum support in `TeamFlags`; kept `forceZDR`/`allowZDR` for backward compat.
  - Introduced helpers to resolve effective mode; search uses `getSearchZDR`, all others use `getScrapeZDR`.
  - Replaced direct flag reads across v0/v1/v2 controllers, libs, and permissions (request-scoped ZDR allowed only when mode is `allowed` or `forced`).
  - Updated tests to the enum format.

- **Migration**
  - Deploy this PR first, then the DB migration (`firecrawl-db#57`), then the cleanup PR to remove legacy boolean support.
  - ACUC cache may serve boolean flags for up to 10 minutes; helpers handle both formats during this window.

<sup>Written for commit 3780cc516badcc3946f10a986f1cec08218e9114. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(scrape): use selectedRow instead of data[0] for GCS fetch and cacheInfo
body:
## Summary

- Fixes a bug where `data[0].id` and `data[0].created_at` were used for the GCS fetch and `cacheInfo` timestamp instead of `selectedRow.id` and `selectedRow.created_at`
- The row selection logic at lines 294-303 may choose a row at index > 0 (e.g. when recent error entries precede a valid 2xx entry but haven't hit the `errorCountToRegister` threshold of 3), causing a mismatch between the selected row and the one actually used for GCS fetch and cache age reporting

## Changes

**`apps/api/src/scraper/scrapeURL/engines/index/index.ts`**
- Line 327: `data[0].id` → `selectedRow.id` (GCS document fetch)
- Line 389: `data[0].created_at` → `selectedRow.created_at` (`cacheInfo` timestamp)

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify cache hits return the correct document when multiple index rows exist for a URL (mix of 2xx and error status codes)
- [ ] Verify `cacheInfo.created_at` reflects the timestamp of the row that was actually selected, not always the first row

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes the index scraper to use the selected row for both the GCS document fetch and cache timestamp. This ensures we fetch the correct document and report the right cache age when the chosen 2xx row isn’t at `data[0]`.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Use `selectedRow.id` for the GCS fetch instead of `data[0].id`.
  - Use `selectedRow.created_at` for `cacheInfo.created_at` instead of `data[0].created_at`.

<sup>Written for commit a47381320a4144c393abea3f225457fd1d4b0886. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

commits.message:
fix(scrape): use selectedRow instead of data[0] for GCS fetch and cacheInfo

The index engine's row selection logic (lines 294-303) may choose a row
other than data[0] — e.g. when recent error entries (403s) precede a
valid 2xx entry but haven't hit the errorCountToRegister threshold of 3.
In that case, data[newest200Index] is selected, but the GCS fetch and
cacheInfo timestamp were still referencing data[0], causing the wrong
document to be fetched and the wrong cache age to be reported.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

title: fix(playwright-service): harden against SSRF
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Hardened the Playwright scraping service against SSRF by validating target URLs, blocking localhost/private IPs (including DNS-resolved private ranges), and returning a 200 with pageStatusCode 403 and a clear error when blocked. Added an opt-in `ALLOW_LOCAL_WEBHOOKS` flag to allow local targets when self-hosting.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Validate URL and protocol; reject `localhost`, `*.localhost`, private IPs, and hostnames that resolve to private IPs (DNS lookup with a short cache).
  - Intercept Playwright requests and abort unsafe ones; track blocked navigation; block service workers.
  - `/scrape` pre-validates targets and, when blocked, returns JSON with `pageStatusCode: 403` and a descriptive `pageError` (HTTP 200).
  - Expose `ALLOW_LOCAL_WEBHOOKS` in Docker Compose and Kubernetes; tests cover allowed vs blocked behavior using `localtest.me`.

- **Dependencies**
  - Added `ipaddr.js`.

<sup>Written for commit ad06d7acde9f034451fba346247152e807786423. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

<file name="apps/playwright-service-ts/api.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/playwright-service-ts/api.ts:62">

When `lookupWithCache` catches a DNS error it returns `[]`, and `[].some(isIPPrivate)` is always `false`, so the URL is treated as safe. title: Replace dorny/test-reporter with inline junit parser
body:
## Summary
- Replaces `dorny/test-reporter@v1` with a shell script that parses junit.xml and writes results to the GitHub step summary
- Gracefully handles missing junit.xml (warns instead of failing)
- Lists failed tests with suite name and failure message
- Works on fork PRs (no `checks:write` permission needed)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Replaced `dorny/test-reporter@v1` with a pure-Python JUnit parser that writes results to the step log and `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`. Removes the temporary CI failures used to validate the reporter.

- **New Features**
  - Parses `apps/api/test-results/junit.xml`, derives totals from testcases, and writes a detailed report to both outputs.
  - Adds emoji tables with a collapsible “All tests”; shows timings, marks skipped tests, and lists failures last in the step log.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Warns (don’t fail) when the report is missing; fails on malformed XML; treats `<failure>` and `<error>` as failures; escapes `|` in names/messages; works on fork PRs.

<sup>Written for commit 03805b88f79575b3b9e6034f66f4b39720ca62ad. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(native): structured tracing and panic safety for Rust PDF processing
body:
## Summary
- Adds `tracing` crate infrastructure to the Rust native module with a scoped log collector and `catch_unwind` panic recovery with full backtrace capture
- Passes `NativeContext` (scrapeId, url) from TypeScript into Rust PDF calls so logs carry trace context
- Emits Rust-side logs through Winston with `{ source: "native", module: "pdf.process" }` labels for filtering
- Wraps PDF Rust calls in Sentry spans (`native.pdf.detect`, `native.pdf.process`)

## Test plan
- [x] All 16 Rust unit tests pass (including 4 new logging tests: log collection, context passing, panic capture, error propagation)
- [ ] Run PDF scrape E2E to verify logs flow through with `source: "native"` labels
- [ ] Verify Sentry spans appear under `native.pdf.*`
- [ ] Test with a corrupted PDF to confirm panic produces backtrace in Sentry instead of worker crash

commits.message:
feat(native): add structured tracing, panic safety, and Sentry reporting for Rust native module

- Add tracing infrastructure (logging.rs) with catch_unwind panic recovery and backtrace capture
- Pass NativeContext (scrapeId, url) from TS to Rust for trace continuity
- Emit Rust logs through Winston with source:"native" labels via emitNativeLogs helper
- Wrap Rust calls in Sentry spans (native.pdf.detect, native.pdf.process, native.document.convert)
- Add missing try-catch + captureExceptionWithZdrCheck to document converter
- Fix unsafe unwrap() calls in html.rs that could crash workers silently
scope PR to PDF only: revert document/html changes

fix: preserve native logs on error/panic paths

TracingResult now wraps the inner Result so logs are always returned.
On NAPI errors, logs are serialized into the error message via
embed_logs_in_error and extracted on the TS side via
extractAndEmitNativeLogs before Sentry reporting.
fix: only strip embedded logs from error message after successful parse

fix(native): change strip to debuginfo to preserve unwind tables

strip="symbols" removes .eh_frame unwind tables, causing catch_unwind
to fail in release builds (SIGABRT instead of recoverable error).
strip="debuginfo" keeps unwind tables while still removing debug info.

title: Add self-hosted OCR A/B experiment for PDF pipeline
body:
## Summary
- Fire-and-forget self-hosted OCR experiment that runs in parallel with MinerU (MU v1) on a configurable percentage of non-cached PDF traffic
- Self-contained in `selfHostedOCR.ts` — single import + single call in `runpodMU.ts`
- Compares both speed and quality against MinerU using a deferred promise pattern
- Logs side-by-side timing (`ocrDurationMs` vs `muV1DurationMs`), markdown lengths, and word-level Jaccard similarity (strips markdown syntax to compare underlying data quality)

## Config
```
PDF_OCR_EXPERIMENT_ENABLE=true
PDF_OCR_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT=10
PDF_OCR_BASE_URL=http://<server-ip>:8000
PDF_OCR_API_KEY=              # optional
```

## Test plan
- Set `PDF_OCR_EXPERIMENT_ENABLE=true` and `PDF_OCR_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT=100`
- Point `PDF_OCR_BASE_URL` at the running OCR API
- Submit a PDF scrape request
- Verify logs show experiment started/completed entries with comparison metrics
- Lower percent to 10 for production rollout

title: (feat/wiki) Wikimedia
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a new `wikipedia` scraping engine using the Wikipedia Enterprise API to fetch clean article HTML across Wikipedia and sister projects. It activates only for Wikimedia URLs and when credentials are set, improving accuracy and speed for those pages.

- **New Features**
  - New `wikipedia` engine (MRT 10s, quality 500); enabled only with `WIKIPEDIA_ENTERPRISE_USERNAME`/`WIKIPEDIA_ENTERPRISE_PASSWORD`.
  - Conditionally added to the engine list and omitted from fallbacks for non-Wikimedia URLs.
  - Language/project-aware URL parsing for Wikipedia, Wiktionary, Wikisource, Wikibooks, Wikiquote, Wikiversity, and Wikivoyage, plus redirect resolution via the MediaWiki API.
  - Caches API tokens in Redis with a distributed lock; auto-clears on 401/403.
  - Returns full article HTML; pipeline supports `markdown`, `html`, `rawHtml`, `links`, and `onlyMainContent`. Tests cover URL parsing, formats, languages, redirects, metadata, and errors.

- **Migration**
  - Optional: set `WIKIPEDIA_ENTERPRISE_USERNAME` and `WIKIPEDIA_ENTERPRISE_PASSWORD` to enable the new engine.

<sup>Written for commit 4927e218d0f9e101cd3ddfb885ae552b115f4a44. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: add query format support to js and python sdks
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Add a new query format to the JS and Python SDKs so you can ask a question about scraped content and get an answer in the response. Tightens Python validation to require a non-empty `prompt` for queries across all input paths.

- **New Features**
  - Accept `QueryFormat` in `formats`: `{ type: 'query', prompt: '...' }`.
  - Responses can include `Document.answer` when using `query`.
  - Python: `QueryFormat` inherits from `Format`, is part of `FormatOption` and `Category`; validation enforces a non-empty `prompt` for `query` (rejects bare `query` strings, dicts/models without `prompt`, and whitespace-only prompts).

- **Dependencies**
  - Bump `@mendable/firecrawl-js` to `4.16.0`.
  - Bump Python `firecrawl` to `4.19.0`.

<sup>Written for commit 3fa0a2e699509e8cf0349f980b2c321d4869180c. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(agent): make maxCredits > 2500 a paid request
body:
## Summary
- When `maxCredits > 2500` is passed via the API, the request is now forced to be a paid request
- This prevents free requests from using credit limits above 2,500
- Dashboard (firecrawl-web) is capped at 2,500; users who need higher limits must use the API

## Changes
- `agent.ts`: After determining free request eligibility, override `isFreeRequest = false` when `maxCredits > 2500`

## Related PRs
- firecrawl/firecrawl-web#1575 — cap maxCredits to 2,500 in dashboard settings modal
- firecrawl/extract-v3 — update error messages to point to API for higher limits

## Test plan
- [ ] Send an agent request with `maxCredits: 3000` on a team with free requests available — should be billed as paid
- [ ] Send an agent request with `maxCredits: 2000` on a team with free requests available — should remain free
- [ ] Send an agent request without `maxCredits` — should behave as before

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Agent API requests with `maxCredits` > 2,500 are always billed, and we now skip free-request consumption in these cases. The dashboard remains capped at 2,500; higher limits require the API.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Skip free request consumption when `maxCredits > 2500` to prevent losing a free request and being billed.

<sup>Written for commit 275e6b5cb7e9bf560686d8d080d371c2d05b2da3. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
feat(agent): make maxCredits > 2500 a paid request

When a user sets maxCredits above 2,500 via the API, the request is
now forced to be a paid request regardless of free request eligibility.
This ensures higher credit limits are only available on paid usage.

test(agent): add unit tests for maxCredits > 2500 paid gating

Verifies that:
- isFreeRequest=true when maxCredits not set and free request consumed
- isFreeRequest=true when maxCredits <= 2500 and free request consumed
- isFreeRequest=false when maxCredits > 2500 (forced paid)
- isFreeRequest=false when maxCredits = 2501 (boundary)
- isFreeRequest=false when no free request available
chore(agent): remove maxCredits unit test file

fix(agent): skip free request consumption when maxCredits > 2500

Move the maxCredits > 2500 check before agent_consume_free_request_if_left
to avoid consuming a free request that will then be forced to paid billing.
Previously the free request was consumed first and then overridden to paid,
causing the user to lose a free request AND get billed.

commits.message: chore: merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into charlie/init-autumn
chore: restore .env.local template with empty values

fix(autumn): address three P1/P2 review issues

Fix 1 (batch_billing): capture the return value of withAuth(supaBillTeam)
and skip Autumn tracking when billing returns success: false without
throwing. Previously the result was discarded, so a Supabase RPC error
would still increment Autumn.

Fix 2 (backfill double-count): pass the current event's credit value into
_backfillUsageIfNeeded and subtract it from the Firecrawl total before
computing the delta. After supaBillTeam commits to the DB, getACUCTeam
returns a total that already includes the current batch; without this
subtraction the backfill would bring Autumn up to that total and the
subsequent track() call would add the same credits again.

Fix 4 (per-mode vs. shared counter): fetch both Scrape and Extract usage
from getACUCTeam in parallel and compare their combined total against
Autumn's single shared TEAM_CREDITS counter. Previously only one mode's
usage was fetched, causing the other mode's history to be silently skipped
during backfill for teams that use both modes.

feat(autumn): add experiment gate with stable UUID-based percent bucketing

Add AUTUMN_EXPERIMENT (on/off) and AUTUMN_EXPERIMENT_PERCENT (0-100)
config flags to gate all Autumn billing calls. The percent gate uses a
deterministic bucket derived from the first 8 hex chars of the org UUID
so the same org always gets the same experiment decision.

All public entry points (reserveCredits, refundCredits,
ensureTeamProvisioned) are gated. Pass orgId to ensureTeamProvisioned
at all call sites to avoid redundant lookups.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): add 5s timeout to Autumn SDK client

Prevents requests from hanging indefinitely if the Autumn API is
unresponsive. Without a timeout the default is -1 (no limit), which
could block awaited callers like extraction-service.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(autumn): guard ensureTeamProvisioned with autumnClient null check

Without this guard, ensureTeamProvisioned makes a Supabase query to
resolve orgId even when autumnClient is null, only for every subsequent
Autumn API call to no-op. The team also never gets cached, so the
wasted query repeats on every call.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: Query
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Add a query format to the v2 scrape pipeline to answer natural-language questions about a page. Returns the answer in answer.

- New Features
  - Add formats: [{ type: "query", prompt: "..." }] to scrape and search scrapeOptions; response includes answer when used.
  - Uses Google Gemini `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` with `gemini-2.0-flash-lite` fallback, with cost tracking and telemetry.
  - Requires page markdown (auto-derived if needed); not supported with zero data retention; graceful warnings on failures.
  - Validates prompt length (max 10,000 chars) and marks request as LLM usage.
  - Billing: 5 credits when query is requested.

- Migration
  - Use formats: [{ type: "query", prompt: "..." }] (optionally include "markdown" to also receive markdown). No top-level query param.
  - Read the answer from answer; field is omitted if query format isn’t included.
  - Keep prompts ≤10,000 characters; each query costs 5 credits.
  - Query is unavailable when zero data retention is enabled.

<sup>Written for commit 4395ec3205b74b5a39dc1bc747fdc1e365ccb26d. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(api): remove dedicated ACUC Supabase client for consistency
body:
## Summary

- Removes `SUPABASE_ACUC_URL`, `SUPABASE_ACUC_SERVICE_TOKEN` config and the `supabase_acuc_only_service` client
- This was a separate Supabase instance used only for ACUC (auth/billing) queries, introduced in Nov 2025 as a temporary measure (had TODO comments to remove it)
- It caused the dashboard to show wrong concurrency limits because `firecrawl-web` calls the same ACUC SQL function against the **main** database while the API used the **dedicated** one, resulting in different return values
- All ACUC queries now go through the main Supabase client (with read replica load balancing), ensuring the API and dashboard always see the same data

## Deployment note

After merging, the `SUPABASE_ACUC_URL` and `SUPABASE_ACUC_SERVICE_TOKEN` environment variables can be removed from deployment config.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify dashboard concurrency circle shows correct limit matching the team's plan
- [ ] Verify usage page browser concurrency chart shows the same limit
- [ ] Verify API authentication still works correctly (rate limiting, credit checks)
- [ ] Confirm no increase in auth latency from switching off dedicated DB

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Removed the dedicated ACUC Supabase client and its env vars. All ACUC queries now use the main Supabase client (with read-replica load balancing) so the API and dashboard see the same data.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Fixed mismatched concurrency limits between the dashboard and API by querying ACUC from the same database.

- **Migration**
  - Remove SUPABASE_ACUC_URL and SUPABASE_ACUC_SERVICE_TOKEN from deployment config.

<sup>Written for commit 8fbd3f2227207db4b6b93908ceb54078eb4b5442. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
fix(api): remove dedicated ACUC Supabase client for consistency

Remove the SUPABASE_ACUC_URL/SUPABASE_ACUC_SERVICE_TOKEN config and the
supabase_acuc_only_service client. This was a separate Supabase instance
used only for ACUC (auth/billing) queries, but it caused the dashboard
to show wrong concurrency limits because firecrawl-web was calling the
same ACUC SQL function against the main database while the API used the
dedicated one, resulting in potentially different return values.

With this change, all ACUC queries go through the main Supabase client
(with read replica load balancing), ensuring the API and dashboard
always see the same data.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: fix(api): pass maxPages to Rust PDF extractor to limit content extraction
body:
## Summary

- **Bug**: When `parsers: [{ type: "pdf", mode: "fast", maxPages: N }]` was used, the `maxPages` parameter was only applied to metadata (`numPages`) and billing, but the Rust PDF extractor (`processPdf()`) still extracted **all pages** of the PDF. This meant users received the full document content while only being charged for N pages.
- **Fix**: Pass `maxPages` to the Rust NAPI binding as `Option<u32>`, which uses `pdf_inspector`'s existing `PdfOptions::pages(1..=N)` page filter to limit text extraction at the source.

### Changes
1. `apps/api/native/src/pdf.rs` — `process_pdf()` now accepts an optional `max_pages` parameter and configures `PdfOptions::pages(1..=n)` when provided
2. `apps/api/src/scraper/scrapeURL/engines/pdf/index.ts` — passes `maxPages` to `processPdf()` call in the Rust extraction path

## Test plan
- [ ] Scrape a large PDF (e.g. 200+ pages) with `parsers: [{ type: "pdf", mode: "fast", maxPages: 10 }]` and verify the markdown content only contains the first 10 pages
- [ ] Verify `numPages` metadata still returns the capped value
- [ ] Verify billing credits match the maxPages value
- [ ] Test without maxPages to confirm no regression (all pages extracted)
- [ ] Test with `mode: "auto"` + maxPages to confirm Rust path still applies the limit

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes a bug where maxPages was ignored by the Rust PDF extractor. Text extraction now stops at the first N pages so content matches billing.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Pass maxPages to processPdf(...) via NAPI as Option<u32>.
  - Apply PdfOptions::pages(1..=N) to limit pages during Rust extraction.

<sup>Written for commit ba6e755b6acfec10440de8f0cef69b259947640f. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
fix(api): pass maxPages to Rust PDF extractor to limit content extraction

The maxPages parameter was only used for metadata/billing in the Rust
extraction path but not passed to the actual processPdf() call. This
meant the full PDF content was returned regardless of maxPages while
only being charged for the limited page count.

Now passes maxPages as a page_filter to pdf_inspector's PdfOptions,
so the Rust extractor only processes the first N pages when specified.

title: feat(api): agent signup v0 endpoints and enforcement
body:
## Summary
- Adds `POST /v2/agent-signup` public endpoint: creates sandboxed account with 50-credit cap, sends sponsor confirmation email, inserts in-app notification
- Adds `POST /v2/agent-signup/confirm` and `POST /v2/agent-signup/block` public endpoints: merge or disable agent key based on sponsor action
- Adds enforcement middleware in `checkCreditsMiddleware` for unverified agent keys (50-credit cap, expiry check, blocked check)
- Adds `agent-sponsor` service with Redis-cached lookups for sponsor status
- Rate limiting: 5 requests/IP/hour, 20 requests/domain/24h (per-email for public providers)

## New files
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/agent-signup.ts` — main signup controller
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/agent-signup-confirm.ts` — confirm/block controllers
- `apps/api/src/services/agent-sponsor.ts` — sponsor lookup + caching service

## Modified files
- `apps/api/src/controllers/auth.ts` — attaches sponsor status to ACUC
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v1/types.ts` — adds `_agentSponsor` field to `AuthCreditUsageChunk`
- `apps/api/src/routes/shared.ts` — enforcement branch in `checkCreditsMiddleware`
- `apps/api/src/routes/v2.ts` — registers new routes
- `apps/api/src/types.ts` — adds `AGENT_SPONSOR_CONFIRM` notification type

## Test plan
- [ ] Call `POST /v2/agent-signup` with valid email → 201 with api_key
- [ ] Verify sandboxed account is created with `agent_provisioned = true`
- [ ] Verify sponsor email is sent via Resend
- [ ] Use returned key for scraping → works within 50-credit cap
- [ ] Exhaust 50 credits → 402 error with `unverified_credit_limit_reached`
- [ ] Call `POST /v2/agent-signup/confirm` with token → key merged to sponsor's account
- [ ] Call `POST /v2/agent-signup/block` with token → key disabled, sandbox banned
- [ ] Verify rate limiting: 6th request from same IP within 1 hour → 429
- [ ] Verify expired deadline → 403 on API usage

## Dependencies
- Requires `firecrawl-db` migration: `eric/implement-this-agent-sign-up-v0-plan`

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds agent signup v0 with public endpoints for sandboxed API keys (50-credit cap) and a sponsor confirm/block flow. Enforces limits, sends sponsor notifications with qs-based links, applies verified status immediately, and records ToS version; verification window is 5 days.

- **New Features**
  - Public endpoints: POST /v2/agent-signup, /v2/agent-signup/confirm, /v2/agent-signup/block.
  - Signup creates a sandboxed key and sponsor record; emails sponsor (and in-app if user exists) with confirm/block links using qs params agent_signup_token and agent_signup_action; stores ToS version and hash.
  - Confirm moves the key to the sponsor’s team or promotes the sandbox, carries over credit_usage, bans the sandbox team, verifies the sponsor, clears sponsor cache, then clears ACUC so the key adopts the verified plan immediately.
  - Block deletes the key, bans the sandbox team, marks sponsor blocked.
  - Enforcement: middleware caps unverified keys at 50 credits, checks expiry/blocked, and attaches sponsor status to ACUC. Rate limits: 5 requests/IP/hour and 20 requests/domain/day (per-email for public domains).

- **Refactors**
  - Centralized PostgREST “no rows” detection via isPostgrestNoRowsError; tightened error handling to return 500 on account creation failure.
  - Strong consistency for blocked/pending sponsor checks via primary Supabase service; added Redis-cached sponsor lookups with cache clear on confirm/block; fixed ToS URL path.

<sup>Written for commit 45c2b2de64013adbb504edf9a368dc433ab20ea4. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: firecrawl-spring

reviews.body:
**3 issues found** across 8 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="apps/api/src/services/agent-sponsor.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/services/agent-sponsor.ts:26">
P2: `JSON.parse(cached)` is outside the `try/catch` block that protects the DB lookup path. If the Redis cache contains corrupt data, this throws an unhandled exception. Wrap the cache deserialization in its own try/catch (or move it inside the existing one) and fall through to the DB lookup on parse failure.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="apps/api/src/services/agent-sponsor.ts:36">
P2: The `verification_token` is a security-sensitive secret (used to confirm/block signups) but is fetched, cached in Redis, and included in the return type of `getAgentSponsorStatus` — even though no consumer of this function uses it. Remove `verification_token` (and the unused `agent_name`, `sandboxed_team_id`) from the SELECT, the type, and the cached object to minimize secret exposure. The token-based lookup is already handled separately by `getAgentSponsorByToken`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="apps/api/src/controllers/v2/agent-signup.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/controllers/v2/agent-signup.ts:68">
P1: IP-based rate limit is trivially bypassable: `x-forwarded-for` is a client-controllable header. An attacker can send a unique `X-Forwarded-For` value with every request to get a fresh rate-limit bucket each time, defeating the 5 req/hr/IP protection entirely.

Additionally, the raw header can be a comma-separated list of IPs (e.g., `"client, proxy1, proxy2"`), making the rate-limit key unreliable even for legitimate traffic.

Use `req.ip` (which respects Express's `trust proxy` setting) or explicitly parse the first/last IP from the forwarde
Need to address https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/pull/2962/changes#r2876533276 and look over the Cubic comments, they make sense imo.

title: feat(api): PDF shadow comparison for Rust
body:
Add fire-and-forget shadow comparison that logs quality metrics (length ratio, number preservation, table counts) when both Rust and MinerU produce output for the same complex PDF, gated behind `PDF_SHADOW_COMPARISON_ENABLE`.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds a fire-and-forget PDF shadow comparison that benchmarks Rust output against MinerU and logs quality metrics. Runs only when both produce markdown and is gated by PDF_SHADOW_COMPARISON_ENABLE.

- **New Features**
  - comparePdfOutputs computes lenRatio, numberPreservationRatio, table counts, and an overall verdict (good/acceptable/poor) with thresholds (good: lenRatio ≥ 0.8 and numbers ≥ 0.9; acceptable: lenRatio ≥ 0.5 and numbers ≥ 0.7).
  - Executes asynchronously after a successful extraction, so it doesn’t affect responses.
  - Logs scrapeId, pageCount, URL (omitted in Zero Data Retention mode), and all metrics.
  - Counts markdown tables by matching header-separator rows for accuracy.
  - Unit tests cover number extraction, table counting, ratios, and verdicts.

- **Migration**
  - To enable, set PDF_SHADOW_COMPARISON_ENABLE=true. Defaults to off.

<sup>Written for commit 052fc20bf622fb1463cb2855ead5051011935bcb. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(rust-sdk): handle metadata fields returned as arrays
body:
The API returns arrays for DocumentMetadata fields (e.g. `"robots": ["index", "follow"]`) when a page has duplicate meta tags, but the Rust SDK types these as `Option<String>`, causing serde to fail with `"invalid type: sequence, expected a string"`.

This adds a custom `deserialize_string_or_array` serde helper that accepts both `String` and `[String]`, joining arrays with `", "`. All `Option<String>` metadata fields in both v1 and v2 DocumentMetadata structs are annotated with it. The public type signature stays `Option<String>` so this is a non-breaking change.

New unit tests cover string values, array values, null, missing fields, empty arrays, single-element arrays, serialization roundtrip, and full document parsing.

Fixes #1304

title: feat(java-sdk): add Maven Central publish workflow
body:
## Summary
- Adds a GitHub Actions workflow (`publish-java-sdk.yml`) that auto-publishes the Java SDK to Maven Central when the version in `build.gradle.kts` is bumped on `main`
- Adds signing and OSSRH repository configuration to `build.gradle.kts`
- Extends the version check script with Java/Gradle support (parses `build.gradle.kts`, queries Maven Central metadata)

## Required Secrets
The following secrets need to be configured in the repo:

| Secret | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `MAVEN_USERNAME` | Sonatype OSSRH username |
| `MAVEN_PASSWORD` | Sonatype OSSRH password/token |
| `GPG_SIGNING_KEY` | ASCII-armored GPG private key |
| `GPG_SIGNING_PASSWORD` | Passphrase for the GPG key |

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify the build still passes (`./gradlew build` in `apps/java-sdk`)
- [ ] Add the 4 secrets to the repo
- [ ] Bump the version in `build.gradle.kts` and merge to trigger a publish
- [ ] Confirm the artifact appears on Maven Central under `com.firecrawl:firecrawl-java`

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

Adds an automated Maven Central publish pipeline for the Java SDK that runs on version bumps to main. Switches to the Vanniktech Maven Publish plugin and Central Portal for simpler signing and release.

  - Replaced manual Gradle publishing/signing with com.vanniktech.maven.publish (Central Portal); set coordinates (com.firecrawl:firecrawl-java), updated POM/SCM URLs to the firecrawl org, and in-memory GPG signing.
  - Extended the version check script to support Java (reads build.gradle.kts, checks Maven Central); corrected example output in its docstring.

- **Migration**

title: feat: audio format (ENG-4708)
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a new "audio" format that returns a signed GCS MP3 URL for YouTube pages via the API and SDKs. Implements ENG-4708 and bills `audio` and `query` as +4 credit add‑ons.

- **New Features**
  - New `audio` format in v2 scrape; available as `document.audio`.
  - Validates only `youtube.com/watch?v=...` and `youtu.be/...`; tests cover success and rejection.
  - Transformer POSTs to `AVGRAB_SERVICE_URL` `/download` to fetch a public MP3 URL; warns and omits `audio` if the service isn’t configured.
  - Ensures `document.audio` only appears when requested; logs a warning if requested but missing.
  - SDKs updated to include `audio`: JS `@mendable/firecrawl-js` 4.18.0, Python `firecrawl` 4.21.0, Rust `firecrawl` 1.4.0, Java SDK 1.2.0.

- **Migration**
  - Set `AVGRAB_SERVICE_URL` in the API environment.
  - Update SDKs to the versions above.
  - Request with `formats: ['audio']` or `{ type: 'audio' }`.
  - Only the YouTube URLs above are supported; others fail validation.

<sup>Written for commit f1b8909d28c36ffdd91586d07f9b0818143629d9. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: feat(sdk): add Java SDK for Firecrawl v2 API
body: ## Summary - Adds a new Java SDK at `apps/java-sdk` for the Firecrawl v2 web scraping API - Follows patterns from Claude and OpenAI Java SDKs (Gradle build, OkHttp, Jackson, builder pattern, CompletableFuture async) - Targets Java 11+ with zero external dependencies beyond OkHttp and Jackson - Supports v2 API methods: scrape, crawl, batch scrape, map, search, agent, browser sandbox, and usage/metrics ## Supported Endpoints | Endpoint | Methods | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | `/v2/scrape` | `scrape`, `scrapeAsync` | Single URL scraping with format options (markdown, HTML, JSON, screenshot, links, branding) | | `/v2/crawl` | `startCrawl`, `getCrawlStatus`, `crawl`, `cancelCrawl`, `getCrawlErrors`, `crawlAsync` | Website crawling with auto-polling and pagination | | `/v2/batch/scrape` | `startBatchScrape`, `getBatchScrapeStatus`, `batchScrape`, `cancelBatchScrape`, `batchScrapeAsync` | Multi-URL batch scraping with idempotency key support | | `/v2/map` | `map`, `mapAsync` | URL discovery on a website | | `/v2/search` | `search`, `searchAsync` | Web search with source/category/location filters | | `/v2/agent` | `startAgent`, `getAgentStatus`, `agent`, `cancelAgent`, `agentAsync` | AI-powered autonomous research agent | | `/v2/browser` | `browser`, `browserExecute`, `deleteBrowser`, `listBrowsers` + async variants | Browser sandbox session management and code execution | | Metrics | `getConcurrency`, `getCreditUsage` | Usage and credit tracking | ## Architecture | Package | Purpose | |---------|---------| | `com.firecrawl.client` | `FirecrawlClient` (main entry point) and internal HTTP client with retry/backoff | | `com.firecrawl.models` | Immutable request options (builder pattern) and response types (Jackson deserialization) | | `com.firecrawl.errors` | Unchecked exception hierarchy: `FirecrawlException`, `AuthenticationException`, `RateLimitException`, `JobTimeoutException` | ## Key Features - **Builder pattern** for all options classes (`ScrapeOptions.builder()`, `CrawlOptions.builder()`, etc.) - **Immutable config objects** with `toBuilder()` for safe modification - **Automatic retry** with exponential backoff (configurable retries and backoff factor) - **Auto-pagination** for crawl and batch scrape results (follows `next` cursor) - **`CompletableFuture` async variants** for all methods (`scrapeAsync`, `crawlAsync`, etc.) - **Environment variable / system property fallback** for API key (`FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`) and URL (`FIRECRAWL_API_URL`) - **Comprehensive error handling** with typed exceptions and HTTP status code mapping ## Updates since last revision ### Devin Review fixes (commit `5a68644`) - **Batch scrape idempotency key**: `idempotencyKey` is now excluded from JSON body via `@JsonIgnore` and sent as the `x-idempotency-key` HTTP header. Added a `post()` overload to `FirecrawlHttpClient` that accepts extra headers. - **Batch scrape flattening order**: When merging scrape options into the request body, batch-level fields (e.g. `urls`) now take priority over nested scrape options, preventing accidental overwrites. ### CI workflow fixes (commit `3528fc0`) - **Restored `dorny/test-reporter`** in `test-server.yml` — this step was accidentally removed on the branch, causing "Test Report" checks to be misattributed to `test-java-sdk.yml` in the GitHub UI. - **Added `dorny/test-reporter`** to `test-java-sdk.yml` to publish Java SDK JUnit test results as a proper GitHub check (`Java SDK Test Report`). ## Test plan - [x] Unit tests for builder construction, validation, and `toBuilder()` immutability - [x] E2E tests (gated on `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY` env var) for scrape, map, crawl, search, concurrency, and credits - [x] Manual live endpoint validation: all 11 tests passed across `/v2/scrape`, `/v2/crawl`, `/v2/search`, `/v2/map`, `/v2/browser`, `/v2/agent`, `/v2/batch/scrape` - [x] Local Gradle build compiles cleanly (`./gradlew build -x test`) - [x] CI checks passing (except pre-existing flaky `should handle invalid prompt gracefully` test in API suite — unrelated to Java SDK) ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human **Risk level: 🟡 Yellow** — Core SDK is solid, but recent fixes need verification: - [ ] **Batch scrape idempotency**: Verify `idempotencyKey` is correctly sent as `x-idempotency-key` HTTP header (not in JSON body). Test with a batch scrape request that includes `idempotencyKey` in `BatchScrapeOptions`. - [ ] **Batch scrape flattening**: Confirm batch-level fields (e.g. `urls`, `webhook`) are not overwritten by nested scrape options. Test with `BatchScrapeOptions` that includes both batch-level and scrape-level fields. - [ ] **CI workflow attribution**: After merge, verify "Test Report" checks appear under the correct workflow names (not misattributed to `test-java-sdk.yml`). - [ ] **Java SDK CI**: Note that `test-java-sdk.yml` won't trigger on this PR (workflow file must exist on `main` first). After merge, verify it runs on subsequent PRs that touch `apps/java-sdk/**`. ### Notes - The failing `should handle invalid prompt gracefully` test in `apps/api/src/__tests__/snips/v2/crawl.test.ts` is a **pre-existing flaky test** (12-minute timeout) unrelated to the Java SDK. It loops through 4 invalid prompts sequentially but the 720s timeout isn't sufficient. - E2E tests are gated on `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY` secret — if not configured, they're silently skipped. All endpoints were manually validated against the live API. - The `test-server.yml` change restores a step from `main` that was accidentally deleted on this branch — no functional changes to the server test suite. --- **Link to Devin run:** https://app.devin.ai/sessions/5b5b5a8cf30b40e5ac51d0ed869fb908 **Requested by:** @Chadha93 --- ## Summary by cubic Adds a new Java SDK (apps/java-sdk) for the Firecrawl v2 API so Java 11+ apps can scrape, crawl, search, run agent tasks, and manage browser sandbox sessions with async support, retries, and typed models. Removes the deprecated extract endpoint; CI runs Java SDK tests on PRs touching apps/java-sdk/** and manual runs, publishes JUnit reports, and fixes misattributed checks by removing the push trigger. Addresses SPT-13. - New Features - Supports v2 endpoints: scrape, crawl, batch scrape, map, search, agent, browser sandbox (create/execute/list/delete), and usage/metrics. - Builder-based, immutable option models with helpers for webhooks, geolocation, and JSON extraction. - Async methods via CompletableFuture and auto-pagination for crawl/batch jobs. - Configurable retry with exponential backoff and typed exceptions. - Env/system property fallback for API key (FIRECRAWL_API_KEY) and base URL. - Bug Fixes - Validate API keys with isBlank() and preserve immutability in ScrapeOptions with defensive copies. - Paginated jobs: null-guard missing data and handle "cancelled" in AgentStatusResponse.isDone to stop polling loops. - MapData: normalize mixed link types (strings and objects) into a uniform representation. - Batch scrape: flatten scrape options to the top-level body; send idempotencyKey via x-idempotency-key header and preserve batch-level fields when merging. - CI/build: pin junit-platform-launcher, include gradle-wrapper.jar, restore/publish JUnit results via dorny/test-reporter in server and Java SDK workflows, and remove the push trigger from test-java-sdk.yml to prevent check misattribution. Written for commit 3ee1fd994465575f87f89761539748772ddb8fd7. Summary will update on new commits. ---
