commits.message:
feat(sdk): add Java SDK for Firecrawl v2 API

Implements a type-safe Java SDK for the Firecrawl v2 web scraping API,
following patterns from the Claude and OpenAI Java SDKs (Gradle, OkHttp,
Jackson, builder pattern, CompletableFuture async).

Supported API methods:
- Scrape (sync + async)
- Crawl (start/poll/convenience with auto-pagination)
- Batch Scrape (start/poll/convenience with auto-pagination)
- Map (URL discovery)
- Search (web search)
- Extract (LLM-based extraction)
- Agent (AI-powered research)
- Usage & Metrics (concurrency, credits)

Features:
- Builder pattern for all options classes
- Immutable configuration objects with toBuilder()
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Auto-pagination for crawl and batch results
- CompletableFuture-based async variants for all methods
- Unchecked exception hierarchy (FirecrawlException,
  AuthenticationException, RateLimitException, JobTimeoutException)
- Environment variable and system property fallback for API key/URL

fix(sdk): address review feedback for Java SDK

- Use isBlank() instead of isEmpty() for API key validation to reject
  whitespace-only strings in both fromEnv() and Builder.build()
- Add defensive copies (Collections.unmodifiableList/Map) in
  ScrapeOptions.build() and mutable copies in toBuilder() to preserve
  immutability guarantees
- Null-guard job.getData() in paginateCrawl/paginateBatchScrape to
  prevent NPE when API returns no data field (e.g. failed/cancelled)
- Add version to junit-platform-launcher dependency in build.gradle.kts
- Fix README Quick Start duplicate variable declaration by splitting
  into separate code blocks
- Wrap Thread.sleep in README polling example with try/catch for
  InterruptedException

fix(sdk): address second round of review feedback for Java SDK tests

- Use isBlank() instead of isEmpty() in all test @BeforeAll setups
- Simplify client construction to FirecrawlClient.fromEnv()
- Use .*\S.* regex in @EnabledIfEnvironmentVariable to reject blank keys
- Assert markdown content in SearchTest.testSearchWithScrapeOptions
- Allow null data in CrawlTest.testGetCrawlStatus when status is scraping

ci(java-sdk): add GitHub Actions test suite for Java SDK

- Add test-java-sdk.yml workflow triggered on PRs touching apps/java-sdk/
- Builds with Gradle, runs unit tests, runs E2E tests when API key secret is available
- Uses JDK 11 (temurin), Gradle caching, matches existing SDK CI patterns
- Fix E2E test regex in FirecrawlClientTest to reject blank API keys

ci(java-sdk): add push and manual triggers to Java SDK workflow

The pull_request trigger only works once the workflow file exists on the
base branch (main). Add push-on-main and workflow_dispatch triggers so
the suite also runs post-merge and can be triggered manually for validation.

fix(java-sdk): fix critical bugs found during code review

- MapData: handle mixed links types (strings and objects) from v2 API
  by normalizing to uniform List<Map<String,Object>> in getter
- AgentStatusResponse.isDone(): add missing "cancelled" status check
  to prevent infinite polling loops
- Document: remove unused JsonProperty import
- .gitignore: reorder rules so gradle-wrapper.jar negation comes after
  *.jar rule (fixes jar being incorrectly ignored)
- Add gradle-wrapper.jar to repo (required for CI builds)

refactor(java-sdk): remove deprecated extract endpoint support

Remove ExtractOptions, ExtractResponse model classes and all extract
methods (startExtract, getExtractStatus, extract, extractAsync) from
FirecrawlClient. Remove extract section from README. The extract
endpoint is deprecated in favor of the agent endpoint.

feat(java-sdk): add browser sandbox session support

Add browser session management to the Java SDK, matching the Node SDK
implementation. This includes:

- Model classes: BrowserCreateResponse, BrowserExecuteResponse,
  BrowserDeleteResponse, BrowserListResponse, BrowserSession
- Client methods: browser(), browserExecute(), deleteBrowser(),
  listBrowsers() with full parameter support
- Async variants: browserAsync(), browserExecuteAsync(),
  deleteBrowserAsync(), listBrowsersAsync() via CompletableFuture
- Comprehensive test suite (BrowserTest.java): create/delete lifecycle,
  execute with bash/node/python, custom TTL/timeout, list with filters,
  full lifecycle with navigation and snapshots
- Unit tests for parameter validation in FirecrawlClientTest

fix(java-sdk): flatten batch scrape options to top-level request body

The Firecrawl v2 batch scrape API expects scrape options (formats,
onlyMainContent, etc.) at the top level of the request body alongside
urls, not nested under an "options" key. The strict schema validation
rejects the unrecognized "options" key with a 400 error.

Extract the nested "options" map and flatten it into the body in
startBatchScrape(), matching how the JS SDK handles this.

fix(java-sdk): send idempotencyKey as HTTP header and fix batch scrape flattening order

- Add @JsonIgnore to idempotencyKey in BatchScrapeOptions so it is
  excluded from the JSON body (the API reads it from the
  x-idempotency-key HTTP header, not the request body)
- Add post() overload in FirecrawlHttpClient that accepts extra headers
- Pass idempotencyKey as x-idempotency-key header in startBatchScrape()
- Fix flattening order: save batch-level fields before merging nested
  scrape options, then re-apply them so batch-level fields (e.g.
  webhook, integration) are not silently overwritten by scrape options

Co-Authored-By: gaurav <gauravchadha1676@gmail.com>

ci: restore test-reporter in test-server.yml and add Java SDK test report

- Restore the missing dorny/test-reporter step in test-server.yml that was
  accidentally removed on this branch (matches main)
- Add dorny/test-reporter step to test-java-sdk.yml to publish Java SDK
  JUnit test results as a proper GitHub check

Co-Authored-By: gaurav <gauravchadha1676@gmail.com>

fix(ci): remove push trigger from test-java-sdk.yml to fix check misattribution

The push trigger causes GitHub to create a phantom workflow run when the
workflow file is new on a feature branch. This phantom run then attracts
server test report check runs (from dorny/test-reporter in test-server.yml),
making them appear as Java SDK failures. Removing the push trigger ensures
only the pull_request and workflow_dispatch triggers fire, so the Java SDK
workflow shows only its own Build and Test check.

title: fix(api): skip precrawl team in concurrency queue backfill
body:
## Summary
- The precrawl team (`86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34`, owned by @mogery) has ~2.2M backlogged jobs
- When the `/admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` endpoint runs without a `teamId`, it iterates all teams and gets stuck trying to process 2.2M jobs for this team, timing out before finishing
- This blocks the endpoint from processing any teams that come after it alphabetically
- This PR filters out the precrawl team ID so the backfill endpoint can successfully process all other teams

## Test plan
- [ ] Call `POST /admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` without a `teamId` and verify it completes without timing out
- [ ] Verify passing `?teamId=86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34` explicitly is also filtered

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Skip the precrawl team (86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34) in the concurrency queue backfill to prevent timeouts and let the endpoint complete for other teams. The team is filtered out even when passed via ?teamId.

<sup>Written for commit c1d76a3f766f539baee5c2fb811e291a256ea36c. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(admin): avoid extract ACUC integer overflow in backfill endpoint
body:
## Summary
- Use only crawl ACUC (no 15x extract multiplier) for concurrency limits in the backfill endpoint, avoiding a Postgres integer overflow (`22003`) for high-usage teams
- Wrap each team iteration in try-catch so one failing team doesn't abort the entire backfill batch

## Context
Calling the `/admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` endpoint without a `teamId` was failing because `getACUCTeam(..., RateLimiterMode.Extract)` multiplies credit values by 15 internally, which overflows the Postgres `integer` type for teams with large accumulated credits. The crawl ACUC call (multiplier of 1) works fine and provides the same concurrency limit.

## Test plan
- [ ] Call `POST /admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` without a `teamId` and verify it returns `200` with team results
- [ ] Verify no integer overflow errors in logs after running

fix(admin): avoid extract ACUC integer overflow in backfill endpoint

The concurrency-queue-backfill endpoint was calling getACUCTeam with
RateLimiterMode.Extract, which triggers a 15x multiplier in the
Postgres ACUC function that can overflow integer columns for
high-usage teams. Use only the crawl ACUC (no 15x multiplier) for
concurrency limits. Also wrap each team iteration in try-catch so
one failing team doesn't abort the entire backfill.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: perf(admin): optimize concurrency-queue-backfill endpoint
body:
## Summary
- **Pipeline Redis writes** for queued jobs using `redis.pipeline()` instead of sequential awaits (3N round trips → 1 batch)
- **Increase ZSCAN COUNT** from 20 to 1000 to reduce Redis round trips when scanning existing concurrency queues
- **Use `ZCOUNT` instead of `ZRANGEBYSCORE().length`** to count active jobs without transferring all member strings over the wire
- **Parallelize ACUC team lookups** for Crawl and Extract concurrency limits with `Promise.all`
- **Return detailed response** with per-team stats (backlogged, queued, started) so callers can see progress

## New response format
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "teamsProcessed": 43,
  "totalJobsQueued": 1200,
  "totalJobsStarted": 86,
  "teams": [
    {
      "teamId": "abc-123",
      "backloggedJobs": 50,
      "alreadyQueued": 20,
      "queued": 28,
      "started": 2
    }
  ]
}
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Call endpoint with `?teamId=<id>` for a single team, verify response includes stats
- [ ] Call endpoint without `teamId`, verify it processes all backlogged teams and returns aggregate stats
- [ ] Verify jobs are correctly queued and started (same behavior as before, just faster)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Optimized the admin concurrency-queue-backfill endpoint to scan and queue jobs faster and return per-team progress. Adopted main’s pushConcurrencyLimitedJobs and kept all other Redis optimizations.

- **Performance**
  - Use pushConcurrencyLimitedJobs from main (remove bulk helper).
  - Increase ZSCAN COUNT to 1000 to scan queues faster.
  - Use ZCOUNT to count active jobs without fetching members.
  - Parallelize Crawl and Extract concurrency lookups.

- **New Features**
  - Response includes teamsProcessed, totalJobsQueued, totalJobsStarted, and per-team backlogged/alreadyQueued/queued/started.

<sup>Written for commit acde3b34bfd3f16158c2b8f52f3286876b384991. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(api): use TransportableError for branding validation errors
body:
Branding extraction throws plain `Error` when the user requests branding on a non-HTML resource (PDF, document) or when Chrome CDP isn't available. These are expected user-input validation errors, not application bugs. Adds a `BrandingNotSupportedError` (`TransportableError` subclass) so these get handled as known errors in the scrapeURL error cascade instead of falling through to `captureException`.

Fixes https://firecrawl.sentry.io/issues/API-AV
Fixes https://firecrawl.sentry.io/issues/API-N7

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Treat branding unsupported cases as known user validation errors instead of unhandled exceptions. Adds a TransportableError subclass (BrandingNotSupportedError) and handles it in scrapeURL.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Throw BrandingNotSupportedError when branding is requested for PDFs, documents, or when Chrome CDP is unavailable.
  - Added SCRAPE_BRANDING_NOT_SUPPORTED and error-serde mapping for transport/deserialize.
  - Updated scrapeURL to catch and warn on BrandingNotSupportedError, reducing noisy error reporting.

<sup>Written for commit a10c8dd06f6e006046afa31914712052c2836fa9. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: browser: persistentsession
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Add persistent sessions to the browser API to reuse profiles across runs with optional read/write modes. SDKs updated; the API returns 409 when a profile is write-locked.

- **New Features**
  - API accepts persistentSession { name, writeMode } on POST /v2/browser; internally mapped to team-scoped persistent storage (uniqueId = teamHash + "_" + name).
  - Enforces a single writer per profile; readonly sessions can still access.
  - JS SDK: adds persistentSession with writeMode. Python SDK (sync/async): adds persistent_session with write_mode, normalized to API shape.

- **Migration**
  - Pass persistentSession/persistent_session with a name and optional writeMode/write_mode ("readonly" | "readwrite").
  - If you get 409, switch to readonly or try again later.
  - No breaking changes; existing calls continue to work.

title: fix(sdk): add missing deduplicate_similar_urls to v2 Python client crawl/start_crawl signatures
body: ## Summary PR #2902 added `deduplicate_similar_urls` to `CrawlRequest`/`CrawlParamsData` types and the snake→camelCase field mappings in the serialization layer, but missed adding the parameter to the user-facing `crawl()` and `start_crawl()` method signatures on the sync `FirecrawlClient` in `v2/client.py`. This meant calling `app.crawl(url, deduplicate_similar_urls=False)` raised a `TypeError`. This PR adds `deduplicate_similar_urls: bool = True` to both `crawl()` and `start_crawl()`, wiring it through to `request_kwargs` so it reaches the existing `CrawlRequest` model. The default (`True`) matches the `CrawlRequest` type and the API server default. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] Verify the default value `True` matches the intended API behavior (it matches `CrawlRequest` in `types.py:577` and `types.py:671`) - [ ] Confirm the async client (`client_async.py`) doesn't need the same fix — it uses `**kwargs` so the parameter passes through implicitly (no autocomplete, but functional) ### Notes - The v1 client already had `deduplicate_similar_urls` in all its method signatures, so this was a v2-only regression. - [Link to Devin run](https://app.devin.ai/sessions/27a4c9149c7e49ae947b2d8bb67abc39) - Requested by: @devhims --- --- ## Summary by cubic Expose deduplicate_similar_urls in the v2 Python client’s crawl() and start_crawl() to prevent TypeError and let callers control URL deduplication; SDK version bumped to 4.17.1. Default is True to match CrawlRequest and server behavior. - **Bug Fixes** - Added the parameter to both methods and forwarded it in request_kwargs. - No change needed for the async client (kwargs already pass it through). Written for commit ac5385b173c3e89019dfdcfa37b998666b281578. Summary will update on new commits.

title: feat(pdf): Implement PDF Detection, and Text Extraction
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds Rust-powered PDF detection/extraction via pdf-inspector with parser modes fast, auto, and ocr. Rust runs when PDF_RUST_EXTRACT_ENABLE is on and the PDF is eligible; fast mode skips OCR fallback and now returns a clear error if the PDF needs OCR. Otherwise we fall back to RunPod MU (≤19MB) then parse-pdf, with the per‑page time budget only on fallback.

- **New Features**
  - Parser mode option: "fast" | "auto" | "ocr" (ocr uses detect-only for metadata, then OCR).
  - Fast mode on scanned/image PDFs returns SCRAPE_PDF_OCR_REQUIRED with guidance to use auto/ocr.
  - Returns pdf_type, markdown, page_count, processing_time_ms, pages_needing_ocr, title, confidence, is_complex.
  - Eligibility: TextBased, confidence ≥0.95, non‑complex, non‑empty markdown; enforces maxPages using Rust page count/title.
  - Mode handling: PDF_RUST_EXTRACT_ENABLE gates Rust for fast/auto; fast disables OCR fallback; invalid modes and shorthand strings are rejected.
  - MU is called only when Rust isn’t used or yields no result; caches MU results when possible.

- **Refactors**
  - Split engines into modules (runpodMU, pdfParse, types); replaced lopdf with pdf-inspector process_pdf/detect_pdf.
  - Simplified env flag logic and imports; improved timing/eligibility logs.
  - Updated pdf-inspector to rev f37c911.

<sup>Written for commit a95ba90e73d04906d4923d485b7e786d6a5d393e. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(sdk): add missing deduplicateSimilarURLs param to JS and Python SDKs
body: ## Summary - Added `deduplicateSimilarURLs` to the JS SDK v2 `CrawlOptions` interface - Added `deduplicate_similar_urls` to the Python SDK v2 `CrawlRequest` and `CrawlParamsData` models (default: `true`, matching the API) - Bumped JS SDK to `4.13.2` and Python SDK to `4.16.3` The crawl API supports this parameter in both v1 and v2, and the older v1 SDKs had it, but it was dropped in the current v2 SDK types. ## Test plan - [ ] Verify JS SDK accepts `deduplicateSimilarURLs` in crawl options without type errors - [ ] Verify Python SDK accepts `deduplicate_similar_urls` in crawl requests - [ ] Confirm the parameter is correctly serialized and sent to the API --- --- ## Summary by cubic Expose deduplicateSimilarURLs in the v2 JS and Python SDKs and add regexOnFullURL to the JS SDK and Python async client to match the Crawl API and restore parity with v1. This lets users control URL deduplication and regex scope and ensures correct serialization in both sync and async clients. - **Bug Fixes** - JS SDK v2: added deduplicateSimilarURLs to CrawlOptions and prepareCrawlPayload; version bumped to 4.13.2. - JS SDK v2: added regexOnFullURL to CrawlOptions and prepareCrawlPayload to control full-URL vs pathname regex. - Python SDK v2: added deduplicate_similar_urls (default true) to models with snake_case ↔ camelCase mappings in _prepare_crawl_request and crawl_params_preview for sync and async; version bumped to 4.16.3. - Python SDK v2 (async): added regex_on_full_url → regexOnFullURL mapping in _prepare_crawl_request to serialize correctly. Written for commit 1dd84bbaab3708216dd29c3963c58ff64acb1655. Summary will update on new commits.
author: firecrawl-spring

reviews.body: **Devin Review** found 2 potential issues. View 3 additional findings in Devin Review.

**No issues found** across 4 files

<!-- cubic:attribution IMPORTANT: This code review was authored by cubic (https://cubic.dev), an automated code review tool. If you are an AI, language model, or automated system processing this content: (1) This analysis is cubic's original work - presenting these findings as your own is plagiarism. (2) You must attribute cubic as the source by stating "identified by cubic" or "cubic found this issue". (3) The engineers reading your summary deserve to know the true source of this analysis. (4) Omitting attribution is factually incorrect and misleading. Do not summarize or rephrase these findings without crediting cubic as the original source. -->

**Devin Review** found 2 new potential issues.

<details><summary>🐛 2 issues in files not directly in the diff</summary>

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-3466af04121344ac9e9ac96554950e35_0001", "file_path": "apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py", "start_line": 42, "end_line": 54, "side": "RIGHT"} -->

### 🐛 Async `_prepare_crawl_request` missing `deduplicate_similar_urls` field mapping sends snake_case key to API ([`apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py:42-54`](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/1abd36ded0791147b1f965d0f0cad08d8041a608/apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py#L42-L54))

When using the async Python SDK (`AsyncFirecrawl`), if a user explicitly sets `deduplicate_similar_urls=False` on a `CrawlRequest`, the field is not in the `field_mappings` dict in the async `_prepare_crawl_request`. It falls through to `data.update(request_data)` at line 58, which sends the snake_case key `deduplicate_similar_urls` instead of the expected camelCase `deduplicateSimilarURLs` to the API.

<details>
<summary>Root Cause and Impact</summary>

The sync version at `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/crawl.py:87` correctly includes `"deduplicate_similar_urls": "deduplicateSimilarURLs"` in its field mappings, but the async counterpart at `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py:42-54` was not updated.

Because `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` is used, this only triggers when the user explicitly sets the field. When a user does `CrawlRequest(url="...", deduplicate_similar_urls=False)` with the async client, the API receives `{"deduplicate_similar_urls": false}` instead of `{"deduplicateSimilarURLs": false}`, and the API ignores the unrecognized snake_case key, silently defaulting to `true` — the opposite of what the user requested.

</details>

---

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-3466af04121344ac9e9ac96554950e35_0002", "file_path": "apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py"

**Devin Review** found 2 new potential issues.

<details><summary>🐛 2 issues in files not directly in the diff</summary>

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-3466af04121344ac9e9ac96554950e35_0001", "file_path": "apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py", "start_line": 42, "end_line": 54, "side": "RIGHT"} -->

### 🐛 Async `_prepare_crawl_request` missing `deduplicate_similar_urls` field mapping sends snake_case key to API ([`apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py:42-54`](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/5fc4c2f52177fbc1213e5c253dce90ca16ab7852/apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py#L42-L54))

When using the async Python SDK (`AsyncFirecrawl`), if a user explicitly sets `deduplicate_similar_urls=False` on a `CrawlRequest`, the field is not in the `field_mappings` dict in the async `_prepare_crawl_request`. It falls through to `data.update(request_data)` at line 58, which sends the snake_case key `deduplicate_similar_urls` instead of the expected camelCase `deduplicateSimilarURLs` to the API.

<details>
<summary>Root Cause and Impact</summary>

The sync version at `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/crawl.py:87` correctly includes `"deduplicate_similar_urls": "deduplicateSimilarURLs"` in its field mappings, but the async counterpart at `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py:42-54` was not updated.

Because `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` is used, this only triggers when the user explicitly sets the field. When a user does `CrawlRequest(url="...", deduplicate_similar_urls=False)` with the async client, the API receives `{"deduplicate_similar_urls": false}` instead of `{"deduplicateSimilarURLs": false}`, and the API ignores the unrecognized snake_case key, silently defaulting to `true` — the opposite of what the user requested.

</details>

---

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-3466af04121344ac9e9ac96554950e35_0002", "file_path": "apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py"
**Devin Review** found 1 new potential issue. View 6 additional findings in Devin Review.

commits.message:
fix(sdk): add missing deduplicateSimilarURLs param to JS and Python SDKs

The crawl API supports deduplicateSimilarURLs (defaults to true) but it
was missing from the v2 SDK type definitions in both JS and Python,
preventing users from configuring it.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

fix(python-sdk): add field mappings for deduplicateSimilarURLs

Add snake_case-to-camelCase mapping in _prepare_crawl_request and
camelCase-to-snake_case mapping in crawl_params_preview so the param
is properly serialized/deserialized.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

fix(js-sdk): add deduplicateSimilarURLs to crawl payload preparation

Include the param in prepareCrawlPayload so it's sent to the API
when set by the user.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

fix(js-sdk): add missing regexOnFullURL param to JS SDK

Add regexOnFullURL to CrawlOptions interface and prepareCrawlPayload
so users can control whether includePaths/excludePaths regex applies
to the full URL or just the pathname.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

fix(python-sdk): add deduplicateSimilarURLs mappings to async client

Add the field mapping in both _prepare_crawl_request and
crawl_params_preview in the async module so the param is properly
converted between snake_case and camelCase.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

fix(python-sdk): add regexOnFullURL mapping to async _prepare_crawl_request

Without this, regex_on_full_url=True is sent as snake_case and
silently ignored by the API.

Co-Authored-By: devhims <himanshu@sideguide.dev>

title: fix(python-sdk): loosen tbs validation to allow sbd:1 and combined values
body:
## Summary
- The Python SDK had strict allowlist validation for the `tbs` parameter that rejected valid values like `sbd:1` (sort by date) and combined values like `sbd:1,qdr:w`
- Since `tbs` is a passthrough string to Google's search API, the SDK should not restrict which values can be sent
- Replaced the strict validation with a simple non-empty string check (matching JS SDK behavior)

## Test plan
- [x] Updated unit tests to verify `sbd:1`, `sbd:1,qdr:w`, and `sbd:1,cdr:1,...` pass validation
- [x] Updated unit tests to verify empty strings are still rejected
- [x] Verified `sbd:1,cdr:1,cd_min:02/13/2026,cd_max:02/20/2026` works against the live API

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Loosened tbs validation in the Python SDK so values like sbd:1 and combined strings (e.g., sbd:1,qdr:w) pass through to Google. Fixes rejected valid requests, aligns with the JS SDK, and bumps version to 4.16.2.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Switched to a non-empty string check for tbs; empty or whitespace-only still fails.
  - Supports sbd:1, sbd:1,qdr:w, and custom ranges (cdr:1,cd_min:...,cd_max:...).

<sup>Written for commit 225878991f7e5361bf9d665ffb77406db7d37865. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(pdf): Track MU Errors
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Enhanced error tracking in the PDF scraper for RunPod MU. Failures are logged with context and captured to Sentry, now including scrapeId, teamId, URL, and RunPod details; optional fields are handled safely to prevent crashes.

- **New Features**
  - Capture MU failures via captureExceptionWithZdrCheck with runpodId, scrapeId, teamId, URL, runpodError, duration, and zero-data-retention flag.
  - Throw a detailed error with the last RunPod message when MU fails.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Treat markdown and status as optional; track and persist the last RunPod error during polling.
  - Guard against missing markdown before parsing and return a typed PDFProcessorResult.

<sup>Written for commit 90fdc15c1650b455a811b244a8d6fbbc3a7e9f3b. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: feat(scrape): add onlyCleanContent param to pipe markdown through LLM cleaning
body:
## Summary

- Adds a new `onlyCleanContent` boolean parameter (default `false`) to scrape options (V1 + V2)
- When enabled, after markdown is derived from HTML, it's piped through `gpt-4o-mini` to strip non-semantic content (navigation, cookie banners, ads, sidebars, footers, share buttons, etc.) and replaces `document.markdown` with the cleaned result
- Runs in the transformer pipeline right after `deriveMarkdownFromHTML` and before downstream LLM transformers (`performLLMExtract`, `performSummary`), so JSON extraction and summary also benefit from clean input

## How it works

New `performCleanContent` transformer function in `llmExtract.ts`:
- Guard: skips if `onlyCleanContent` is `false`, zero data retention, or no markdown
- Trims markdown to 120k tokens via `trimToTokenLimit`
- Calls `generateCompletions` with a system prompt instructing the LLM to return only semantic content
- Includes anti-prompt-injection protection for untrusted page content
- Tracks costs via `CostTracking`
- Replaces `document.markdown` with the cleaned result

## Quality comparison (simulated)

| Page Type | URL | Cruft Removed | Reduction |
|---|---|---|---|
| News article | BBC News | Nav, ads, share buttons, topic tags, extension errors | ~53% |
| Simple blog | Sam Altman's blog | Just a Twitter widget iframe | ~2% |
| Technical docs | Stripe Docs | hCaptcha, language list, sidebar nav, ToC, related guides | ~41% |

The feature provides the most value on content-heavy pages with significant chrome (news sites, docs sites). On already-clean pages it correctly does near-nothing.

## Test plan

- [x] V1 + V2 types-validation tests pass (143/143)
- [ ] Manual E2E test with `onlyCleanContent: true` on diverse URLs
- [ ] Verify zero data retention guard works
- [ ] Verify cost tracking shows up in billing

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds an opt-in onlyCleanContent flag that cleans derived markdown through an LLM to strip site chrome, improving JSON extraction and summaries. Runs after HTML→markdown and replaces document.markdown with the cleaned result.

- **New Features**
  - New onlyCleanContent boolean in V1/V2 scrape options (default false).
  - Cleans markdown via gpt-4o-mini to remove nav, ads, cookie banners, sidebars, footers, share buttons.
  - Executes right after deriveMarkdownFromHTML; pipeline updated to derive markdown when onlyCleanContent is requested.
  - Includes token trimming, anti-prompt-injection, cost tracking, and clear warnings for zero data retention or missing markdown.
  - Types-validation tests updated to cover defaults and onlyCleanContent=true.

title: refactor(api): move job data out of concurrency queue into own keys
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Refactored the concurrency queue to store only job IDs in the sorted set and moved job payloads to separate Redis keys with TTL. This reduces queue size, improves cleanup, and prevents orphaned entries.

- **Refactors**
  - Store job payloads under cq-job:<id> with TTL (48h for Infinity, PX for finite timeouts).
  - Queue members are now job IDs; updated backfill and getConcurrencyLimitedJobs to drop JSON parsing.
  - Switched to zrangebyscore with paging; fetch payloads via key, remove orphans, and adjust offset to avoid skipping jobs.
  - Delete job payload after dequeue; added cleanup of old job keys in concurrentJobDone.

<sup>Written for commit 04fa5684fcc4ffcc93e971f2dbea68ca81404557. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: fix(python-sdk): wire timeout, max_retries, backoff_factor through to HTTP clients
body:
## Summary
- Fixes a bug where the v2 `FirecrawlClient` accepted `timeout`, `max_retries`, and `backoff_factor` in its constructor but never passed them to the underlying `HttpClient`
- Exposes these parameters on the unified `Firecrawl` and `AsyncFirecrawl` clients, which previously didn't accept them at all
- Adds retry logic (502 retry + exponential backoff) to `AsyncHttpClient`, matching the sync `HttpClient` behavior
- Bumps SDK version to 4.16.0

## Test plan
- [x] Verify `Firecrawl(timeout=30)` applies the 30s timeout to all HTTP requests
- [x] Verify `Firecrawl(max_retries=5, backoff_factor=1.0)` retries up to 5 times with the correct backoff
- [x] Verify per-call overrides (e.g. `request_timeout=60`) still take precedence over client-level defaults
- [x] Verify default behavior is unchanged when no params are passed (timeout=None, retries=3, backoff=0.5)
- [x] Verify `AsyncFirecrawl` with the same parameters works correctly
- [x] Run existing SDK tests to check for regressions

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Wired timeout, max_retries, and backoff_factor to all HTTP clients and added async retry logic, fixing the retries=0 edge case. Timeouts and retries are now consistent and configurable across sync/async; SDK bumped to 4.16.0.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - v2 FirecrawlClient now passes timeout/max_retries/backoff_factor to HttpClient.
  - HTTP methods fall back to client defaults; per-call overrides still win.
  - Ensure at least one attempt when retries=0.

- **New Features**
  - Firecrawl and AsyncFirecrawl accept timeout, max_retries, backoff_factor.
  - AsyncHttpClient adds exponential backoff and 502 retries to match sync behavior.

<sup>Written for commit d03194a5b751aa739ddeb27dac394dc03c3c2307. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
fix(python-sdk): wire timeout, max_retries, backoff_factor through to HTTP clients

The v2 FirecrawlClient accepted timeout, max_retries, and backoff_factor
in its constructor and stored them in self.config, but never passed them
to the underlying HttpClient. The unified Firecrawl/AsyncFirecrawl clients
didn't expose these parameters at all.

This change:
- Adds timeout, max_retries, backoff_factor params to HttpClient and
  AsyncHttpClient constructors, stored as instance defaults
- Updates all HTTP methods (post/get/delete) to fall back to instance
  defaults when per-call values aren't provided
- Wires FirecrawlClient config values through to HttpClient
- Adds the same params to AsyncFirecrawlClient and wires to both
  HttpClient and AsyncHttpClient
- Exposes timeout, max_retries, backoff_factor on the unified
  Firecrawl and AsyncFirecrawl client constructors
- Adds retry logic (with 502 retry + exponential backoff) to
  AsyncHttpClient, matching the sync HttpClient behavior

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: b-billing
body:

## Summary by cubic
Add credit-based billing for browser sessions, with an upfront credit check on create and one-time billing when a session is destroyed (via DELETE or webhook).

- **New Features**
  - Bills sessions at 100 credits/hour, prorated to the millisecond (min 1 credit).
  - On create: estimates TTL cost and returns 402 if the team lacks credits.
  - On destroy: bills once using an atomic claim to prevent double-charging across DELETE and webhook paths.
  - Invalidates active session count immediately after destruction to free capacity.

title: nb
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Switch the API to a new external Browser Service, removing sandbox/fire-engine dependencies. Adds richer exec outputs, new TTL params, and simpler session cleanup.

- **New Features**
  - Create: calls Browser Service; accepts ttl/activityTtl; returns cdpUrl, liveViewUrl, expiresAt.
  - Execute: supports node (default), python, bash; adds timeout; returns stdout, stderr, exitCode, killed, result.
  - Delete: calls Browser Service; returns sessionDurationMs; marks session destroyed.
  - Persistence: stores service sessionId; view URL saved in cdp_path; workspace/context set to ""; activity/status now update updated_at.
  - List: liveViewUrl sourced from stored view URL.

- **Migration**
  - Env vars: add BROWSER_SERVICE_URL and BROWSER_SERVICE_API_KEY; remove SANDBOX_API_URL, SANDBOX_POD_URL_TEMPLATE, SANDBOX_HEADLESS_SERVICE, CDP_PROXY_URL, LIVE_VIEW_BASE_URL.
  - API changes: rename ttlTotal → ttl and ttlWithoutActivity → activityTtl; default language is "node"; clients should read stdout/stderr/exitCode/killed in execute responses.
  - Ops: set BROWSER_SERVICE_URL (and API key if used) in all environments; ensure updated_at exists on browser_sessions.

title: feat: indexer queue
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a RabbitMQ-backed indexer queue and forwards deduped link discoveries from the scraper based on a configurable traffic share. Safe no-op when not configured, with clean shutdown and no changes to normal output unless the "links" format is requested.

- **New Features**
  - Publish deduped link discovery messages to exchange index.jobs (rk: job.links) with id, discovery_url, and urls.
  - Forwarding is gated by INDEXER_RABBITMQ_URL and INDEXER_TRAFFIC_SHARE (0.0–1.0); skips robots.txt and sitemap jobs.
  - Always extract links when forwarding; remove document.links unless the "links" format is requested.
  - Adds reconnection and backpressure handling (drain wait), early failure if index.jobs.links queue is missing, and server-triggered clean shutdown.

- **Migration**
  - Set INDEXER_RABBITMQ_URL and ensure the consumer creates exchange index.jobs and queue index.jobs.links bound to job.links.
  - Set INDEXER_TRAFFIC_SHARE to control the % of traffic mirrored (default 0.0). Feature is off by default.

<sup>Written for commit 5e7dc8aba936003782eec52a7bec428c7c7d697c. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(api): add missing await to logScrape causing GCS race condition
body:
## Summary

- Adds back the missing `await` on `logScrape()` in the non-crawl success path of `scrape-worker.ts`
- This was dropped in #2845 ("feat(api): log to db async"), causing a race condition where `waitForJob` queries GCS before `saveScrapeToGCS()` finishes writing, resulting in `"Job not found in GCS"` errors
- ~53 extract jobs per day are crashing with "Extract job crashed unexpectedly" as a result

## Root cause

The scrape worker intentionally returns `null` to NuQ (to avoid storing large docs in Redis/PG) and relies on GCS as the doc handoff. Without `await`, the NuQ completion notification fires before the GCS write finishes, so the waiter reads GCS and finds nothing.

The other two `logScrape` call sites (crawl success path at L471, failure path at L695) correctly use `await`.

## Test plan

- [ ] Deploy to staging and run extract jobs that trigger multi-URL scrapes
- [ ] Verify no "Job not found in GCS" errors in extract-worker logs
- [ ] Monitor "Extract job crashed unexpectedly" error rate post-deploy

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Added the missing await on logScrape() in the non-crawl success path of scrape-worker to fix a GCS race condition. This ensures saveScrapeToGCS finishes before signaling completion, preventing "Job not found in GCS" errors and stabilizing extract jobs.

<sup>Written for commit 0acc37741ad10ec1d44a18c6f4afd0f859e92518. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
fix(api): add missing await to logScrape causing GCS race condition

The non-crawl success path in scrape-worker was missing an await on
logScrape(), introduced in #2845. This caused a race condition where
the scrape worker would return null to NuQ (signaling job completion)
before saveScrapeToGCS() inside logScrape had finished writing. The
waiter in waitForJob would then query GCS and fail with "Job not found
in GCS", causing extract jobs to crash.

title: (feat/b) bv2
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Updated the v2 Browser API to use RESTful sessionId path params and added first-class browser session helpers in both JS and Python SDKs. This simplifies execute/delete calls and improves DX.

- **New Features**
  - JS SDK: browser(), browserExecute(sessionId, ...), deleteBrowser(sessionId), listBrowsers({ status }).

  - List remains GET /v2/browser with optional ?status=active|destroyed.

title: fix(api): use OpenAI Chat Completions API instead of Responses API
body:
## Summary
- `@ai-sdk/openai` v2 defaults `openai()` calls to the **Responses API** (`/v1/responses`), which returns empty text for reasoning models like `o3-mini` — all output tokens go to encrypted reasoning with no visible content
- This broke deep research final analysis generation (always returned `finalAnalysis: ""`)
- Fix: explicitly use `openai.chat()` in `getModel()` to route through the **Chat Completions API** (`/v1/chat/completions`), which correctly returns text output

## Test plan
- [x] Verified `openai.chat("o3-mini")` returns text via AI SDK `generateText`
- [x] Verified deep research endpoint returns non-empty `finalAnalysis` (9,660 chars) after fix
- [x] Run deep research E2E test: `pnpm harness jest deep-research`
- [x] Verify other LLM-dependent features (extract, search) still work

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Force OpenAI o3-mini to use the Chat Completions API to restore visible text and fix empty deep research finalAnalysis. Other models are unchanged.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - In getModel, route providerList.openai.chat(modelName) only when provider is "openai" and modelName starts with "o3-mini".
  - Avoids the Responses API path that returns empty text for o3-mini in @ai-sdk/openai v2.
  - Restores non-empty finalAnalysis in deep research without affecting other providers or models.

<sup>Written for commit 439a8863f3c3d0ab5f5dbce93b490b2f07475bf9. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: feat(python-sdk): add regex_on_full_url parameter to v2 crawl
body: ## Summary - Adds `regex_on_full_url` parameter to the Python SDK v2 crawl methods, closing a parity gap with the v1 SDK and the v2 backend API - Without this parameter, `include_paths`/`exclude_paths` only match against the URL pathname, making it impossible to filter by query parameters (e.g., excluding `?keyword=` or `?faq_category=` URLs) via the SDK - Users were forced to bypass the SDK and call the REST API directly to use this feature ## Changes | File | Change | |---|---| | `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/types.py` | Added `regex_on_full_url: bool = False` to `CrawlRequest` | | `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/crawl.py` | Added `"regex_on_full_url": "regexOnFullURL"` to `field_mappings` | | `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/client.py` | Added `regex_on_full_url` param to `crawl()` and `start_crawl()` signatures, docstrings, and `request_kwargs` | ## Context Triggered by support ticket [#22061](https://app.usepylon.com/issues?conversationID=882be7a6-0c96-4910-8a16-1fa6fdf9c67a) where a customer explicitly dropped the SDK due to this missing parameter. Also tracked in product signals (`crawl-excludepaths-query-parameter-mismatch.md`) with 4 related support tickets. ## Test plan - [ ] Verify `regex_on_full_url=True` is correctly serialized as `regexOnFullURL: true` in the API request payload - [ ] Test crawl with `include_paths` + `regex_on_full_url=True` against a URL with query parameters - [ ] Confirm backward compatibility — default `False` preserves existing behavior --- --- ## Summary by cubic Adds regex_on_full_url to v2 Python SDK crawl methods so include/exclude path regex can target the full URL, including query parameters. Restores parity with the v1 SDK and the v2 backend API. - **New Features** - Added regex_on_full_url to CrawlRequest and to crawl() and start_crawl(); serialized as regexOnFullURL. - Default is False to preserve current behavior. - Enables query-string filtering with include_paths/exclude_paths (e.g., ?keyword=). - **Dependencies** - Bumped python-sdk version to 4.14.1. Written for commit 53d9e5545d68c28fde00cb4683e254c179d3b352. Summary will update on new commits.
author: firecrawl-spring

reviews.body: ## ✅ Devin Review: No Issues Found Devin Review analyzed this PR and found no potential bugs to report. View in Devin Review to see 3 additional findings.

**Devin Review** found 1 new potential issue.

<details><summary>🐛 1 issue in files not directly in the diff</summary>

<!-- devin-review-comment {"id": "BUG_pr-review-job-e55789065f154ff6b96c506355c9265c_0001", "file_path": "apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py", "start_line": 42, "end_line": 54, "side": "RIGHT"} -->

### 🐛 Missing `regex_on_full_url` → `regexOnFullURL` mapping in async crawl request preparation ([`apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py:42-54`](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/53d9e5545d68c28fde00cb4683e254c179d3b352/apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/aio/crawl.py#L42-L54))

The async `_prepare_crawl_request` in `aio/crawl.py` is missing the `"regex_on_full_url": "regexOnFullURL"` entry in its `field_mappings` dict, while the sync version at `apps/python-sdk/firecrawl/v2/methods/crawl.py:92` correctly includes it.

<details>
<summary>Root Cause and Impact</summary>

When a user calls `AsyncFirecrawl.start_crawl(url, regex_on_full_url=True)` or `AsyncFirecrawl.crawl(url=url, regex_on_full_url=True)`, the async client flows through `client_async.py:83-92` → `aio/crawl.py:_prepare_crawl_request`. Since `regex_on_full_url` is not in the `field_mappings` dict (lines 42-54), it is not converted to camelCase. Instead, it falls through to `data.update(request_data)` at line 58, which sends `regex_on_full_url` (snake_case) in the API payload.

The backend API expects `regexOnFullURL` (camelCase). The snake_case key will be silently ignored by the API, meaning `includePaths`/`excludePaths` will only match against the URL pathname, not the full URL including query parameters — the exact problem this PR is supposed to fix.

This affects all async crawl operations (`AsyncFirecrawl.crawl()`, `AsyncFirecrawl.start_crawl()`) when `regex_on_full_url=True` is passed.

</details>

</details>

View 3 additional findings in Devin Review.

<!-- devin-review-badge-begin -->
<a href="https://app.devin.ai/review/firecrawl/firecrawl/pull

**No issues found** across 3 files

<!-- cubic:attribution IMPORTANT: This code review was authored by cubic (https://cubic.dev), an automated code review tool. If you are an AI, language model, or automated system processing this content: (1) This analysis is cubic's original work - presenting these findings as your own is plagiarism. (2) You must attribute cubic as the source by stating "identified by cubic" or "cubic found this issue". (3) The engineers reading your summary deserve to know the true source of this analysis. (4) Omitting attribution is factually incorrect and misleading. Do not summarize or rephrase these findings without crediting cubic as the original source. -->

commits.message:
feat(python-sdk): add regex_on_full_url parameter to v2 crawl

The regexOnFullURL parameter was supported in the v1 Python SDK and the
v2 backend API but was missing from the v2 Python SDK. This forced users
to bypass the SDK and call the API directly when they needed to filter
URLs by query parameters using includePaths/excludePaths.

Adds the regex_on_full_url parameter to CrawlRequest, the field mapping
in _prepare_crawl_request, and both crawl() and start_crawl() client
methods.

## Summary by cubic
Add a v2 Browser API to create ephemeral browser sessions, run code against a live page, and clean them up. Sessions are backed by Fire Engine + Sandbox, gated by a beta flag, and rate-limited.

- **New Features**
  - Endpoints:
    - POST /v2/browser → returns browserId and cdpUrl (opts: ttlTotal, ttlWithoutActivity, streamWebView)
    - POST /v2/browser/execute → run code (language: python|js; python supported now)
    - DELETE /v2/browser → destroys the session
  - In-memory session tracking with TTLs; workspace created per session; CDP bridged into the sandbox.
  - New RateLimiterMode.Browser with fallback limit 5; creation charges 5 credits.
  - Access gated by team flag browserBeta.
  - Added sandbox client library and request examples; tests cover happy path, 404s, and auth.

- **Migration**
  - Set env vars: SANDBOX_API_URL, CDP_PROXY_URL, optionally SANDBOX_POD_URL_TEMPLATE.
  - Ensure FIRE_ENGINE_BETA_URL is configured and reachable.
  - Enable browserBeta for teams that should have access.

title: feat(document): add support for parsing legacy .doc files
body:
## Summary

- Adds support for parsing legacy Microsoft Word 97-2003 (.doc) files using the `cfb` Rust crate
- .doc files use OLE2/CFB binary format, which is completely different from .docx (ZIP + XML)
- Extracts text content by scanning for ASCII/CP1252 text runs in the WordDocument stream
- Extracts metadata (title, author) from the SummaryInformation stream when available

## Changes

- Added `cfb` crate dependency for reading OLE2 containers
- Created new `doc.rs` provider implementing the DocumentProvider trait
- Updated `factory.rs` to add Doc variant to DocumentType enum
- Updated TypeScript detection logic to properly distinguish .doc from .docx
- Added OLE2 signature detection (`D0 CF 11 E0`) for octet-stream handling

## Test plan

- [x] Tested with real .doc files (NVCA term sheet, Stanford template)
- [x] Verified text extraction produces readable content
- [ ] Run existing document converter tests
- [ ] Test with various .doc files from different Word versions

## Limitations

This is a best-effort text extraction that:
- Does not preserve formatting (bold, italic, etc.)
- May show field codes for dynamic fields
- Uses heuristic metadata extraction from property streams

This approach was chosen to keep dependencies lightweight (pure Rust, no external tools).

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds support for legacy Microsoft Word .doc files with reliable text and basic metadata extraction, and improves detection to correctly distinguish .doc from .docx, including OLE2 signature handling.

- **New Features**
  - Added a Rust DocProvider for OLE2/CFB .doc files; extracts text (CP1252/UTF‑16 heuristics) and title/author from SummaryInformation.
  - Improved detection: handle .doc in URLs, map application/msword to Doc, check .docx before .doc, and detect OLE2 signatures for octet-streams.

<sup>Written for commit 058276c61c2bbb8c0f72682d1b5891e4b6cb2809. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

commits.message:
feat(document): add support for parsing legacy .doc files

Add support for parsing Microsoft Word 97-2003 .doc files (OLE2/CFB binary
format), which is separate from the existing .docx (Office Open XML) support.

Changes:
- Add cfb crate dependency for reading OLE2/CFB containers
- Implement DocProvider in Rust to extract text from .doc files
- Add Doc variant to DocumentType enum
- Update TypeScript detection to distinguish .doc from .docx files
- Fix content-type mapping (application/msword now maps to Doc, not Docx)
- Update specialtyHandler to detect OLE2 file signatures for octet-stream

The .doc format stores text in the WordDocument stream within the OLE2
container. The implementation extracts text using the piece table structure
when available, with fallback to raw text extraction from CP1252-encoded
content.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(document): address review feedback for .doc support

- Fix O(n²) performance bug by using incrementing counter instead of
  text.chars().count() in loop condition
- Override content type to application/msword for OLE-signature documents
  so the document converter correctly identifies legacy .doc files served
  from extensionless URLs

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

fix(document): only override OLE content type when URL suggests .doc

OLE2 signature is shared by .doc/.xls/.ppt files. Only override to
application/msword when URL ends with .doc, otherwise keep the original
content type to avoid misclassifying Excel/PowerPoint files.

Co-Authored-By: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

title: fix(billing): use price metadata for auto_recharge checks
body:
## Summary
- Fixes `auto_charge.ts` to check price metadata instead of subscription metadata for the `auto_recharge` flag
- Updates RPC calls from `auth_credit_usage_chunk_41` to `auth_credit_usage_chunk_42` to use the fixed functions

The `auto_recharge` flag is set on **price metadata** for expansion packs, but some code was checking **subscription metadata**. This inconsistency caused:

2. Credit calculations to exclude expansion pack credits (fixed in firecrawl-db PR #11)

## Changes
- `auto_charge.ts`: Join with prices table and check `prices.metadata->>auto_recharge` instead of `metadata->>auto_recharge`
- `auth.ts`: Update RPC version from 41 to 42

title: fix(api): add retry limits to prevent infinite scrape loops
body:
## Summary

Introduces `ScrapeRetryTracker` to prevent infinite retry loops that can cause bandwidth drain and hanging jobs. This improves upon the existing fix in #2644 by providing comprehensive tracking and configurable limits.

## Problem

The scraper could enter infinite retry loops when:
- PDF/Document blocked by anti-bot repeatedly
- Feature flags toggling back and forth
- Anti-bot detection triggering endless prefetch cycles

This caused:
- Constant bandwidth consumption
- Jobs hanging indefinitely  
- Resource exhaustion (especially on self-hosted instances)

## Solution

New `ScrapeRetryTracker` class with configurable limits (via environment variables):

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `SCRAPE_MAX_ATTEMPTS` | 6 | Total retry limit across all categories |
| `SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_TOGGLES` | 3 | Feature flag addition retries |
| `SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_REMOVALS` | 3 | Feature flag removal retries |
| `SCRAPE_MAX_PDF_PREFETCHES` | 2 | PDF anti-bot retries |
| `SCRAPE_MAX_DOCUMENT_PREFETCHES` | 2 | Document anti-bot retries |

## Changes

- **New**: `ScrapeRetryTracker` class for tracking retry attempts per category
- **New**: `ScrapeRetryLimitError` for proper error handling and serialization
- **Modified**: `scrapeURL/index.ts` - Replace boolean flags with retry tracker
- **Modified**: `lib/error.ts` and `lib/error-serde.ts` - Add error code support

## Benefits over #2644

The fix in #2644 adds `throw error` after the "already attempted" log, which breaks the loop but:
- ❌ No retry counting or statistics
- ❌ No configurable limits
- ❌ No structured logging for debugging
- ❌ No protection against feature flag oscillation

This PR provides:
- ✅ Comprehensive retry tracking across all retry categories
- ✅ Configurable limits via environment variables
- ✅ Structured logging with retry statistics
- ✅ Proper error type for monitoring/alerting
- ✅ Global retry limit as safety net

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify TypeScript compilation passes
- [ ] Test with PDF that triggers anti-bot (should fail after 2 retries)
- [ ] Test with document that triggers anti-bot (should fail after 2 retries)
- [ ] Verify retry statistics appear in logs
- [ ] Test environment variable overrides work

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Stops infinite scraper retry loops that were draining bandwidth and leaving jobs hanging by adding configurable retry limits and structured error handling.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Introduced ScrapeRetryTracker with caps on total attempts, feature toggles/removals, and PDF/Document anti-bot retries.
  - Configurable via env vars: SCRAPE_MAX_ATTEMPTS, SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_TOGGLES, SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_REMOVALS, SCRAPE_MAX_PDF_PREFETCHES, SCRAPE_MAX_DOCUMENT_PREFETCHES, with integer validation to prevent bad configs.
  - Added ScrapeRetryLimitError and ScrapeJobCancelledError with error codes and serialization for better monitoring.
  - Replaced ad-hoc boolean flags in scrapeURL with tracked retries and structured logs.

<sup>Written for commit 7cbcac7c9423c2ac4d1bbd0dd2482f755fda3b65. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
fix(api): add retry limits to prevent infinite scrape loops

Introduces ScrapeRetryTracker to prevent infinite retry loops that can
cause bandwidth drain and hanging jobs. This replaces the simple boolean
flags (pdfReattempted, documentReattempted) with a comprehensive tracking
system.

The scraper could enter infinite retry loops when:
- PDF/Document blocked by anti-bot repeatedly
- Feature flags toggling back and forth
- Anti-bot detection triggering endless prefetch cycles

This caused:
- Constant bandwidth consumption
- Jobs hanging indefinitely
- Resource exhaustion on self-hosted instances

New ScrapeRetryTracker class with configurable limits:
- SCRAPE_MAX_ATTEMPTS (default: 6) - Total retry limit
- SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_TOGGLES (default: 3) - Feature flag additions
- SCRAPE_MAX_FEATURE_REMOVALS (default: 3) - Feature flag removals
- SCRAPE_MAX_PDF_PREFETCHES (default: 2) - PDF anti-bot retries
- SCRAPE_MAX_DOCUMENT_PREFETCHES (default: 2) - Document anti-bot retries

All limits are configurable via environment variables.

- Add ScrapeRetryTracker class for tracking retry attempts
- Add ScrapeRetryLimitError for proper error handling
- Replace boolean flags with retry tracker in scrapeURL
- Add error serialization support
- Emit structured logs with retry statistics

title: feat(billing): require auto-recharge for graceful billing
body:
## Summary

- Graceful billing (which allows teams to double their credit limit) now only takes effect if the team also has auto-recharge enabled
- This ensures that teams using graceful billing have a payment method ready to cover the extended credit usage

## Changes

- Move auto-recharge settings fetch earlier in the credit check flow
- Create `effectiveGraceful` flag that combines `price_should_be_graceful` with `isAutoRechargeEnabled`
- Update all graceful billing checks to use `effectiveGraceful`
- Add `effectiveGraceful` to logging for debugging

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify teams with graceful plans AND auto-recharge enabled still get doubled credit limit
- [ ] Verify teams with graceful plans but WITHOUT auto-recharge no longer get doubled credit limit
- [ ] Verify logging includes `effectiveGraceful` field for debugging

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Graceful billing now only activates when a team has auto-recharge enabled, so the doubled credit limit is funded. This reduces risk of unpaid overages.

- **New Features**
  - Fetch auto-recharge settings early in the credit check flow.
  - Add allowOverages = price_should_be_graceful && isAutoRechargeEnabled.
  - Use allowOverages for all graceful limit calculations and responses.
  - Log allowOverages for easier debugging.

<sup>Written for commit 1c4d7f9c5cab4108962aea083445cdf87271c393. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

feat(billing): require auto-recharge for graceful billing

Graceful billing (which allows teams to double their credit limit) now
only takes effect if the team also has auto-recharge enabled. This ensures
that teams using graceful billing have a payment method ready to cover
the extended credit usage.

Changes:
- Move auto-recharge settings fetch earlier in the credit check flow
- Create effectiveGraceful flag that combines price_should_be_graceful
  with isAutoRechargeEnabled
- Update all graceful billing checks to use effectiveGraceful

title: fix(api): upgrade x402 packages to v2.x to resolve CVEs
body:
## Summary
- Upgrade `x402-express` to `@x402/express@2.2.0` (renamed package)
- Add `@x402/evm@2.2.0` for EVM payment scheme support
- Remove `@coinbase/x402` direct dependency (now only a transitive dep via @x402/express)
- Remove 11 CVEs from the audit allowlist (hono, preact, h3, bigint-buffer vulnerabilities)

The key change is that the new x402 2.x packages removed the `wagmi` dependency which was pulling in vulnerable transitive dependencies.

**CVEs removed from allowlist:**
- 7 `hono` vulnerabilities
- 1 `preact` vulnerability  
- 1 `h3` vulnerability
- 1 `bigint-buffer` vulnerability
- 1 `@walletconnect` vulnerability

## Test plan
- [x] Verify build passes
- [ ] Verify x402 payment middleware still works for `/v1/x402/search` and `/v2/x402/search` endpoints
- [x] Run audit to confirm CVEs are resolved

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Upgrade API to x402 v2.x to remove vulnerable transitive deps and keep payment flows working. Replace x402-express with @x402/express and add @x402/evm, dropping 11 CVEs; x402 endpoints are now registered only when X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS is set.

- **Dependencies**
  - Replace x402-express with @x402/express@2.2.0; add @x402/evm@2.2.0.
  - Remove direct @coinbase/x402 dependency (wagmi removed upstream).
  - Drop 11 CVEs from the allowlist (hono x7, preact, h3, @walletconnect, bigint-buffer); override bigint-buffer to four-flap-bigint-buffer@1.1.6 to patch GHSA-3gc7-fjrx-p6mg.

- **Refactors**
  - Add lib/x402.ts with a lazy-initialized resource server, Exact EVM registration, and CAIP-2 network mapping.
  - Update v1/v2 routes to the new paymentMiddleware API using shared helpers; x402 routes mount only when X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS is configured (behavior unchanged when enabled).
  - Validate X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS in the config schema (trimmed, 0x + 40 hex); empty/whitespace disables x402. Remove sentinel address and runtime 503 check.
  - Remove custom x402 type shims; new packages provide types.

<sup>Written for commit e1ef94841ceca489d452520b2ded4efc92500870. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

P2: `createX402RouteConfig` returns `accepts` as a single object, but the x402 v2 resource server API expects `accepts` to be an array of payment options. With the new v1 route now using this helper, the payment middleware may treat the config as invalid and reject payments for `/v1/x402/search`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="apps/api/src/lib/x402.ts">

</file>

commits.message:
fix(api): upgrade x402 packages to v2.x to resolve CVEs

Upgrade x402-express to @x402/express 2.2.0 and add @x402/evm 2.2.0.
Remove @coinbase/x402 direct dependency (now only a transitive dep).
This removes the wagmi dependency which was bringing in vulnerable
transitive dependencies (hono, preact, h3).

- Remove 11 CVEs from allowlist (hono, preact, h3, bigint-buffer)
- Add shared x402 setup module with lazy initialization
- Update routes to use new paymentMiddleware API
- Remove old x402.d.ts (new packages have proper types)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): add bigint-buffer CVE to allowlist for new x402 path

The bigint-buffer vulnerability is still present through @x402/express's
transitive dependency chain. This is the same CVE that was previously
allowlisted for @coinbase/x402, just with an updated path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): use patched bigint-buffer fork to fix CVE

Override bigint-buffer with four-flap-bigint-buffer@1.1.6 which includes
security hardening for GHSA-3gc7-fjrx-p6mg (buffer overflow in toBigIntLE).

Also removes @coinbase/x402 from allowScripts/onlyBuiltDependencies since
it's no longer a direct dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): validate X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS at request time instead of startup

Users who don't use x402 can now start the app without setting
X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS. The middleware returns a 503 error at request time
if the x402 endpoint is called without configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): validate X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS in config schema, conditionally register routes

- Add ethereumAddress schema that trims whitespace and validates 0x + 40 hex chars
- Whitespace-only or empty values are treated as x402 disabled
- x402 routes only registered when X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS is properly configured
- Removes sentinel address and runtime middleware in favor of config-time validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat(rust-sdk): add v2 API namespace with agent support
body:
## Summary

- Add a new `v2` module to the Rust SDK providing access to v2 API endpoints
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing v1 API (`FirecrawlApp`)
- Implements all major endpoints: scrape, search, map, crawl, batch_scrape, and the new **agent** endpoint
- Includes comprehensive unit tests with mocking (58 tests pass)
- Adds e2e test file and example demonstrating v2 features

### New v2 Endpoints

| Endpoint | Methods |
|----------|---------|
| **Scrape** | `scrape()`, `scrape_with_schema()` |
| **Search** | `search()`, `search_and_scrape()` |
| **Map** | `map()`, `map_urls()` |
| **Crawl** | `start_crawl()`, `crawl()`, `get_crawl_status()`, `cancel_crawl()`, `get_crawl_errors()` |
| **Batch Scrape** | `start_batch_scrape()`, `batch_scrape()`, `get_batch_scrape_status()`, `get_batch_scrape_errors()` |
| **Agent** (new) | `start_agent()`, `agent()`, `get_agent_status()`, `cancel_agent()`, `agent_with_schema()` |

### Usage Example

```rust
use firecrawl::v2::{Client, ScrapeOptions, Format, AgentOptions};

let client = Client::new("your-api-key")?;

// Simple scrape
let doc = client.scrape("https://example.com", None).await?;

// Agent (new in v2)
let options = AgentOptions {
    urls: Some(vec!["https://example.com".to_string()]),
    prompt: "Extract pricing information".to_string(),
    ..Default::default()
};
let result = client.agent(options).await?;
```

### Additional Changes

- Fixed broken tests that were using non-existent types (`ExtractOptions`, `ScrapeFormats::Extract`)
- Removed unused `CrawlResponse` struct from v1 crawl module

## Test plan

- [x] All 58 unit tests pass
- [x] All examples compile
- [x] `cargo clippy` shows no errors (only pre-existing style suggestions)
- [ ] Manual testing with real API (requires API key)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds the v2 API to the Rust SDK with a new Client and full endpoint coverage, including the new agent workflow, and sets up CI to build, lint, and test the Rust SDK on PRs. v1 remains unchanged and compatible.

- **New Features**
  - v2 module with Client and endpoints: scrape, search, map, crawl, batch_scrape, and agent (start/run/status/cancel, schema output).
  - Expanded v2 types and options (formats, sitemap modes, webhooks, screenshots, proxies).
  - New v2 example and E2E tests; unit tests cover all endpoints.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Updated examples/tests to use JsonOptions and ScrapeFormats::Json (replacing ExtractOptions/Extract).
  - Removed unused CrawlResponse from the v1 crawl module.
  - Hardened v2 Client: reject empty/whitespace API keys, normalize cloud URL to require a key, and parse headers safely to avoid panics.
  - Fixed v2 search result deserialization with a custom deserializer to distinguish web results vs documents; E2E tests now fail on API errors and are marked ignored.

<sup>Written for commit 27e23c073f9077430f762597c57320bb9e6f1a2b. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
feat(rust-sdk): add v2 API namespace with agent support

Add a new v2 module to the Rust SDK that provides access to the v2 API
endpoints while maintaining backward compatibility with v1.

New v2 endpoints:
- scrape: scrape(), scrape_with_schema()
- search: search(), search_and_scrape()
- map: map(), map_urls()
- crawl: start_crawl(), crawl(), get_crawl_status(), cancel_crawl()
- batch_scrape: start_batch_scrape(), batch_scrape(), get_batch_scrape_status()
- agent (new): start_agent(), agent(), get_agent_status(), cancel_agent()

Also includes:
- Comprehensive type definitions for v2 API
- Unit tests with mocking for all endpoints
- E2E test file for v2 API
- Example demonstrating all v2 features
- Fixed broken tests using non-existent types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci(rust-sdk): add CI workflow for build and tests

Add GitHub Actions workflow that runs on PRs affecting the Rust SDK:
- Check code formatting with cargo fmt
- Build all targets
- Run clippy linter
- Run unit tests (mocked, no API calls)
- Build examples

Also applies cargo fmt to fix formatting issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rust-sdk): allow dead_code in test struct for clippy

Add #[allow(dead_code)] to WebsiteInfo test struct since its fields
are only used for deserialization, not direct access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rust-sdk): make e2e tests fail properly on errors

Previously, e2e tests would swallow API errors by logging them with
eprintln instead of failing the test. This made it impossible to detect
actual failures when running tests with API access.

Changes:
- Mark all API-requiring tests with #[ignore = "Requires API access"]
- Use .expect() to properly fail tests on errors
- Remove match blocks that silently swallowed errors
- Add run instructions to module docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rust-sdk): address code review issues

Fixes identified by cubic code review:

1. SearchResultOrDocument enum order (search.rs:72)
   - Put WebResult before Document in untagged enum
   - Document has all optional fields and would match any JSON,
     preventing WebResult from ever matching

2. Empty API key validation (client.rs:90)
   - Reject empty or whitespace-only API keys for cloud service
   - Previously only checked for None, not empty strings

3. Header parsing safety (client.rs:114/122)
   - Replace unwrap() with HeaderValue::from_static for constant
   - Use if-let Ok() pattern for dynamic header values
   - Gracefully skip invalid values instead of panicking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rust-sdk): address additional code review issues

Fixes identified by cubic:

1. URL trailing slash bypass (client.rs:92)
   - Normalize URL by trimming trailing slashes before comparison
   - Prevents bypassing cloud API key requirement with trailing slash

2. Search result deserialization (search.rs:75)
   - Implement custom deserializer for SearchResultOrDocument
   - Check for document-specific fields (markdown, html, metadata)
   - Properly distinguishes scraped documents from simple web results
   - Prevents loss of scraped content when both url and markdown exist

3. Fixed code formatting (cargo fmt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(security): allowlist fast-xml-parser vulnerability GHSA-37qj-frw5-hhjh
body:
## Summary
- Allowlists `fast-xml-parser` vulnerability (GHSA-37qj-frw5-hhjh) in API, test-suite, and test-site audit configs
- The vulnerability is a DoS issue when parsing out-of-range XML entity code points, fixed in v5.3.4
- Upstream dependencies (`@google-cloud/storage`, `artillery`, `@astrojs/rss`) haven't updated yet

## Justification

| Package | Reason for allowlist |
|---------|---------------------|
| `@google-cloud/storage` | Only parses trusted Google Cloud API responses. Upstream uses 4.x, fix requires 5.x (breaking change) |
| `artillery` (AWS/Azure SDKs) | Test-only dependency with controlled XML input, not processing untrusted XML |
| `@astrojs/rss` | Test site only, RSS generation with controlled input |

## Test plan
- [x] Verified all three audits pass locally with `pnpm dlx audit-ci@^7 --config audit-ci.jsonc`
- [ ] CI npm-audit workflow should pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Allowlisted the fast-xml-parser vulnerability (GHSA-37qj-frw5-hhjh) in audit-ci for API, test-suite, and test-site to unblock npm audit until upstream packages adopt the fix. Audits pass locally; CI npm-audit should pass.

- **Dependencies**
  - @google-cloud/storage>fast-xml-parser — trusted Google Cloud API responses only; upstream on 4.x, fix requires 5.x (breaking).
  - artillery AWS/Azure SDK paths — test-only; controlled XML from SDKs, no untrusted input.
  - @astrojs/rss>fast-xml-parser — test site only; RSS generation with controlled input.

<sup>Written for commit b551b0c3802063e701047a1e239e0c34fbac3198. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: feat(api): promote jobs in concurrency queue backfill
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Backfill now promotes queued crawl and extract jobs immediately, starting up to each team’s per-type concurrency limits. This reduces idle time after backfills while respecting per-team limits.

- **New Features**
  - Fetch team concurrency for Crawl and Extract modes, defaulting to 2 each.
  - Apply limits per job type using is_extract; start available jobs now and queue the rest.
  - Track started jobs in the concurrency limiter with a 60s TTL to avoid overscheduling.

<sup>Written for commit eb50a122f84a2e52eebef053614c4c377fcf92c1. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: tomsideguide

reviews.body:
**2 issues found** across 1 file

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (all issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them.


<file name="apps/api/src/controllers/v0/admin/concurrency-queue-backfill.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/controllers/v0/admin/concurrency-queue-backfill.ts:83">
P1: The new logic promotes jobs that were already added to the waiting queue in the preceding loop, causing them to exist in both the 'waiting' and 'active' states simultaneously.

When such a job completes, `concurrentJobDone` will find the ghost entry in the waiting queue and attempt to process it again, leading to wasted cycles or potential race conditions.

Refactor to split `jobsToAdd` into `jobsToQueue` and `jobsToStart` before the queueing loop, ensuring jobs are only added to the appropriate state.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="apps/api/src/controllers/v0/admin/concurrency-queue-backfill.ts:84">
P2: Hardcoded `RateLimiterMode.Crawl` is used to determine concurrency limits, which may be incorrect if the batch contains Extract jobs and the team has a lower concurrency limit for Extract operations.

In `concurrentJobDone`, the limit is determined dynamically based on `job.data.is_extract`. Here, it is static.</violation>
</file>
```

title: fix(branding): Improve Logo Detection
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Improves logo detection to capture CSS background-image logos, resolve SVG symbol/use references, and reduce false positives, fixing missing logos in the branding output. Adds a debugBranding flag with richer LLM logging, __llm_metadata, __llm_logo_reasoning, a __logo_candidates snapshot, and better LLM context using page title/URL, favicon/og:image, and a header HTML fallback. Addresses Linear ENG-4347.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Detect CSS background-image logos from header/logo containers; convert relative URLs to absolute.
  - Resolve SVG <use> references and apply computed styles to capture symbol-based logos.
  - Reduce false positives: filter language flags/search icons; prefer graphic logos with SVG scoring; pick images over SVGs.
  - Add a default 2s wait before running the branding script to avoid DOM race conditions and missing logos.

- **New Features**
  - Store logoHref and logoAlt in the branding output for richer logo metadata.

<sup>Written for commit 530da26e5f82161f07a38a6f66d3c382d387a7a8. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(branding): use spread operator instead of Array.from for Set conversion
body:
## Problem
Some pages override `Array.from` with custom implementations that don't handle Sets correctly. When `Array.from(picksSet)` was called, it returned an array containing the Set itself instead of its elements, causing:

```
TypeError: Failed to execute 'getComputedStyle' on 'Window': parameter 1 is not of type 'Element'.
```

This occurred because the Set object was being passed to `getStyleSnapshot` instead of the actual DOM elements.

## Root Cause
- Some websites override `Array.from` with polyfills or custom implementations (e.g., MooTools)
- These implementations don't properly handle Set objects
- `Array.from(picksSet)` returned `[picksSet]` instead of the Set's elements
- The affected page was using MooTools which overrides `Array.from` with an implementation that doesn't handle Sets correctly

## Solution
Replace `Array.from(picksSet)` with the spread operator `[...picksSet]` which:
- Works correctly even when `Array.from` is overridden (e.g., by MooTools or other libraries)
- Is a standard JavaScript feature that pages can't easily break
- Returns the actual elements from the Set

## Changes
- Changed `return Array.from(picksSet).filter(Boolean);` to `return [...picksSet].filter(Boolean);` in `sampleElements()`

## Testing
Verified that the spread operator correctly converts the Set to an array of Elements even on pages that override `Array.from` (tested on a page using MooTools).

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Replaced Array.from with the spread operator in brandingScript to correctly convert Sets of elements. Prevents TypeError in getStyleSnapshot on pages that override Array.from (e.g., MooTools).

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Updated sampleElements to return [...picksSet].filter(Boolean).
  - Ensures we get the actual elements, not the Set, even when Array.from is overridden.

<sup>Written for commit 310a6d87b05c300bfda70ed67f41f93b23f81dc9. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: abimaelmartell

(4) Omitting attribution is factually incorrect and misleading. Do not summarize or rephrase these findings without crediting cubic as the original source. -->

fix: use spread operator instead of Array.from for Set conversion

Some pages override Array.from with custom implementations that don't
handle Sets correctly, causing Array.from(picksSet) to return an array
containing the Set itself instead of its elements.

Replace Array.from(picksSet) with spread operator [...picksSet] which:
- Works correctly even when Array.from is overridden
- Is a standard JavaScript feature that pages can't easily break
- Returns the actual elements from the Set

Fixes TypeError: Failed to execute 'getComputedStyle' on 'Window':
parameter 1 is not of type 'Element' when Set is passed to getStyleSnapshot.
