title: Filter non-HTTP(S) protocols with separate error message
body:
# Filter non-HTTP(S) protocols with separate error message

## Summary

Firecrawl was attempting to crawl `telnet://` links and other non-web protocols. This PR adds filtering for non-HTTP(S) protocols with a dedicated error message, separate from the existing social media error.

**Changes:**
- Added `NON_WEB_PROTOCOL` denial reason in both Rust and TypeScript
- Created filtering logic to skip URLs with protocols: `mailto:`, `tel:`, `telnet:`, `ftp:`, `ftps:`, `ssh:`, `file:`
- Moved `mailto:` from social media filtering to non-web protocol filtering
- Implemented checks in both Rust (primary) and JavaScript (fallback) code paths
- Added test to verify non-web protocols are filtered during crawls
- Fixed unrelated knip issue (removed unused `hasAnyFormatOfTypes` export)

The filtering happens early in the link processing pipeline, before depth and file type checks, and applies to both `_filter_links` and `_filter_url` functions.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Verify CI tests pass** - The new test `"filters out non-web protocol links"` should pass and confirm that crawled pages only contain http/https URLs
- [ ] **Test error message distinction** - Manually verify that non-web protocol links (e.g., `telnet://example.com`, `mailto:test@example.com`) are filtered with the new "URL uses a non-web protocol" error message, not the social media error
- [ ] **Check protocol list completeness** - Confirm that the list of filtered protocols (mailto, tel, telnet, ftp, ftps, ssh, file) is complete, or identify any missing protocols that should be added
- [ ] **Verify Rust/JS consistency** - Ensure both the Rust implementation (`is_non_web_protocol`) and JavaScript fallback (in `crawler.ts`) filter the exact same set of protocols

### Test Plan
1. Start a crawl on a site that contains links with non-web protocols (or create a test page)
2. Verify that none of the crawled results contain non-HTTP(S) URLs
3. Check the denial reasons to confirm they use the new NON_WEB_PROTOCOL message
4. Test with each protocol specifically: telnet://, ftp://, ssh://, file://, mailto:

### Notes
- The test couldn't be run locally due to environment issues (missing Go dependency), so CI is the first real verification
- `mailto:` was moved from the social media filter to the non-web protocol filter for better semantic grouping
- Both Rust (primary filtering path) and JavaScript (fallback when Rust fails) implementations were updated to ensure consistent behavior

Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/9207f01754e948bcb655e54c9653caed
Requested by: Micah Stairs (@micahstairs)

title: Implement Branding Format
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds a new "branding" output format (ENG-3817) that runs on-page JavaScript and server-side processing to extract brand identity (logo, fonts, colors, typography, and UI tokens). Clients can request this format and receive a typed branding profile in scrape results.

- **New Features**
  - API: Adds "branding" to v1/v2 formats; returns Document.branding with a typed BrandingProfile and updates OpenAPI.
  - Engine: Executes a branding script via Chrome CDP when requested; parses returns and attaches them to the document; allows media; enforces CDP engine availability.
  - Transformers: Process raw payload into a BrandingProfile with LLM-assisted button classification, color roles, and font cleanup; enforce presence only when requested.
  - Index: Skips index caching when "branding" is requested.
  - BrandingScript: Collects raw CSS values, element snapshots, typography, images (logo/favicon/og), framework hints, color scheme, and resolves SVG styles in-page.
  - Samples: Adds branding.requests.http for local testing.

- **Migration**
  - To use, include "branding" in formats.
  - Clients should accept the new enum value and optional branding field in responses.
  - Scrapes with "branding" will bypass index caching.

<sup>Written for commit 9f757ad4b0a7b1eb168cad9481c896e1a6d72942. Summary will update automatically on new commits.</sup>

## Summary by cubic
Enhances HTML-to-Markdown conversion to correctly handle <pre> and inline <code>, extracting nested content and generating fenced code blocks with language hints. Addresses Linear ENG-3806 where nested code and PRE within HTML were not detected.

- **Bug Fixes**

  - Improves inline code outside PRE: extracts text safely and uses longer backtick fences when needed (no escaping).

- **Dependencies**

title: fix: import MAX_MAP_LIMIT from types.ts to resolve 1000 URL cap
body:
## Description
Fixes #2319 

The `/v2/map` endpoint was incorrectly capped at returning only 1,000 URLs, even when users requested higher limits (up to 5,000 or 100,000).

## Root Cause
`apps/api/src/lib/map-utils.ts` had its own hardcoded `MAX_MAP_LIMIT = 1000` constant on line 26, which was shadowing the correct `MAX_MAP_LIMIT = 100000` defined in `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/types.ts`.

## Solution
- Removed hardcoded `const MAX_MAP_LIMIT = 1000` from `map-utils.ts`
- Added `MAX_MAP_LIMIT` to the import statement from `../controllers/v2/types`
- Now uses the correct limit of 100,000 (single source of truth)

## Changes Made

**Before:**
```typescript
import {
  TeamFlags,
  MapDocument,
  scrapeOptions,
  ScrapeOptions,
} from "../controllers/v2/types";

const MAX_MAP_LIMIT = 1000; **// Hardcoded wrong limit**
```

**After:**
```typescript
import {
  TeamFlags,
  MapDocument,
  scrapeOptions,
  ScrapeOptions,
  MAX_MAP_LIMIT, **// Imported from types.ts**
} from "../controllers/v2/types";

// Hardcoded constant removed
```



<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes the /v2/map endpoint incorrectly capping results at 1,000 by importing MAX_MAP_LIMIT from types.ts, enabling up to 100,000 URLs. Fixes #2319.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Removed hardcoded MAX_MAP_LIMIT = 1000 from map-utils.ts.
  - Import MAX_MAP_LIMIT from controllers/v2/types (100000) as the single source of truth.
  - Allows requests for higher limits to return full results.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix(nuq,concurrency-limit): handle if there are duplicate jobs in the concurrency queue
body:
This happened due to a Redis-related outage.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fix duplicate jobs in the concurrency queue by making job promotion and insertion idempotent, preventing re-adds and stuck promotions during outages.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Concurrency: retry promotion up to 10 times, skip when the job already exists, and bail with a warning if no promotable job is found.
  - Queue (NuQ): added addJobIfNotExists with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; promoteJobFromBacklogOrAdd now guards against duplicates and may return null.
  - Maintains team concurrency limits, respects crawl delay, and adds clearer logs and metrics.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: mogery

reviews.body:
**2 issues found** across 2 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (all 2 issues)</summary>

~~~

Understand the root cause of the following 2 issues and fix them.


<file name="apps/api/src/services/worker/nuq.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/services/worker/nuq.ts:749">
When ON CONFLICT skips the insert, result is null but we still set nuq.job_created to true, so telemetry reports duplicates as newly created jobs. Please base the attribute on whether a row was inserted.</violation>
</file>

<file name="apps/api/src/lib/concurrency-limit.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/lib/concurrency-limit.ts:363">
When promotion fails for a duplicate job, the active job entry added earlier is never removed. This leaves a phantom active slot that lowers the available concurrency for up to a minute, causing follow-on jobs to be throttled unnecessarily.</violation>
</file>
~~~

</details>

title: feat: new crawl architecture
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Introduces a new Postgres-backed crawl architecture with grouped jobs, reliable completion handling, and simpler, faster status reporting. Improves self-host support and makes local dev setup easier.

- **New Features**
  - Crawl groups with TTL and lifecycle tracking (addGroup, getGroup).
  - Dedicated crawl-finish queue and worker to send completion webhooks and log jobs.
  - Status endpoints now read numeric stats and listings from NuQ, with zero-data-retention awareness.
  - Dev harness auto-starts a nuq-postgres container (Docker/Podman) if NUQ_DATABASE_URL is not set.

- **Migration**
  - Apply apps/nuq-postgres/nuq.sql to create new tables and cron jobs.
  - Set NUQ_DATABASE_URL (and NUQ_DATABASE_URL_LISTEN) in production environments.
  - No API changes; controllers now create a crawl group at start.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: mogery

reviews.body:
**7 issues found** across 14 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (all 7 issues)</summary>

~~~

Understand the root cause of the following 7 issues and fix them.


<file name="apps/api/src/services/extract-worker.ts">

<violation number="1" location="apps/api/src/services/extract-worker.ts:280">
The worker loop in `crawlFinishWorker` duplicates the generic `NuQJob` processing pattern found in `apps/api/src/services/worker/nuq-worker.ts`. The logic for job acquisition, lock renewal, and job status updates should be abstracted into a common utility function that takes the queue instance and a job processing callback.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="apps/api/src/services/extract-worker.ts:309">

title: feat(nuq): group_id, job backlogs, and group add operations
body:
preliminary work for crawl overhaul
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Introduces job backlogs, owner/group IDs, and bulk add/promote operations to the NuQ scrape queue to improve crawl concurrency and grouping. Concurrency-limited jobs now go to a backlog and are promoted when capacity frees up.

- **New Features**
  - Backlog storage for scrape jobs with promote-from-backlog support.
  - Bulk insert APIs: addJobs for both active and backlogged queues.
  - Job metadata: ownerId (team_id) and groupId (crawl_id) persisted; non-UUID ownerIds normalized via uuid v5.
  - Concurrency integration: concurrency-limited jobs are backlogged; completed jobs promote the next pending backlog entry.
  - Schema updates: owner_id and group_id on nuq.queue_scrape; new nuq.queue_scrape_backlog table.

- **Migration**
  - Apply apps/nuq-postgres/nuq.sql to add columns and create the backlog table.
  - Run pnpm install (uuid upgraded to 13.0.0).

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

Inserting into the backlog table reuses `this.jobReturning`, but that array includes columns like status/finished_at/lock that the backlog table does not have, so any `backlogged` insert will fail at runtime with `column ... does not exist`.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="apps/api/src/services/worker/nuq.ts:727">

Bulk-enqueued concurrency-limited jobs are added without setting `concurrencyLimited`, so the worker never shows the throttling warning for these jobs. Please spread the job data and set `concurrencyLimited: true` before enqueueing.</violation>

~~~

title: feat(nuq): dynamically calculate current concurrency
body:
Tracking current concurrency via the _owner_concurrency and _group_concurrency tables led to a lot of dead tuples and lock contention. Calculating it dynamically should improve performance under load.
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Compute current concurrency on the fly by counting active jobs per owner/group. Removes counter updates and locking, cutting dead tuples and improving performance under load.

- **Refactors**
  - Replace current_concurrency with dynamic COUNT(*) in activation queries (per-owner and per-owner-per-group).
  - Add indexes for active owner/group counts to keep queries fast.
  - Remove all reads/writes to current_concurrency in activate/complete/fail paths.
  - Drop the cron job that synced current_concurrency; keep max_concurrency sync only.
  - Simplify SQL to select and activate jobs without locking owner/group rows.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commits.message:
Fix search pricing bug: charge 2 credits for 1-10 search results without scraping

Previously, the pricing logic for search without scraping was incorrect
in v1 and v2 APIs:
- v1: charged (results * 2) / 10 credits (e.g., 5 results = 1 credit)
- v2: charged (results * 2) / 10 credits (e.g., 5 results = 1 credit)

This fix updates both API versions to charge a flat 2 credits
for 1-10 search results when scraping is not requested, which is
the correct pricing model.

Co-Authored-By: Micah Stairs <micah.stairs@gmail.com>

Fix search pricing formula: use Math.ceil(count / 10) * 2

Updated the pricing calculation for search without scraping to use
the correct formula: Math.ceil(totalResultsCount / 10) * 2

This means:
- 1-10 results = 2 credits
- 11-20 results = 4 credits
- 21-30 results = 6 credits
etc.

Previously used incorrect formula (results * 2) / 10 which charged
1 credit for 5 results instead of 2 credits.

Co-Authored-By: Micah Stairs <micah.stairs@gmail.com>

title: Update /search endpoint pricing to 2 credits per 10 search results
body:
# Update /search endpoint pricing to 2 credits per 10 search results

## Summary
Changed the pricing calculation for the `/search` endpoint from **1 credit per search result** to **2 credits per 10 search results** (effectively 0.2 credits per result, rounded up using `Math.ceil`).

**What changed:**
- Updated pricing formula in both v1 and v2 search controllers
- Only affects searches with NO scraping (when `scrapeOptions.formats` is empty/undefined)
- When scraping IS requested, existing scraping cost calculation remains unchanged
- Formula: `Math.ceil((resultCount * 2) / 10)`

**Example billing:**
- 1-5 results: 1 credit
- 6-10 results: 2 credits  
- 11-15 results: 3 credits
- 20 results: 4 credits

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
- [ ] **Verify the pricing formula is correct** - Confirm `Math.ceil((count * 2) / 10)` matches the intended business requirement for "2 credits per 10 search results"
- [ ] **Test with real API calls** - Make actual `/v1/search` and `/v2/search` requests with various result counts (1, 5, 10, 11, 20) and verify billing is accurate
- [ ] **Check edge cases** - Test with 0 results, 1 result, exactly 10 results to ensure ceiling behavior is correct
- [ ] **Consider adding automated tests** - No tests were added for this pricing change; consider if test coverage should be added before merging

### Test Plan
```bash
# Test v2 search endpoint with no scraping
curl -X POST https://api.firecrawl.dev/v2/search \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query": "test", "limit": 5}'

# Check creditsUsed in response - should be 1 for 5 results
# Test with limit: 10 - should be 2 credits
# Test with limit: 11 - should be 3 credits
```

### Notes
- **⚠️ Financial Impact**: This PR changes billing calculations. Any errors could result in incorrect customer charges.
- **No Automated Tests**: Manual calculation testing was performed, but no test suite coverage was added.
- Code changes are minimal and focused - only the billing calculation formula was modified.
- Documentation was updated in both v1 and v2 docs to reflect the new pricing.

---
**Link to Devin run**: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/4a1be5b6f93a4699a87e03d8c3ccba92  
**Requested by**: Micah Stairs (@micahstairs)

---

## Summary by cubic
Updated /search pricing to 2 credits per 10 results for searches without scraping, replacing 1 credit per result; scraping pricing remains unchanged. Applies to both v1 and v2 controllers and uses Math.ceil((resultCount * 2) / 10) for billing.

title: python-sdk: Fix timeout handling across api calls
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Fixes timeout handling in the Python SDK by adding a request_timeout that is correctly passed to all crawl status requests (sync and async), including pagination. Addresses ENG-3750 by preventing hanging status checks and aligning per-request timeouts with expectations.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Added request_timeout to client.crawl, client.get_crawl_status, async wait_crawl/get_crawl_status, and underlying methods; forwarded to HTTP client get calls.
  - Applied request_timeout to all paginated page fetches (sync/async).
  - Default per-request timeout to request_timeout, or fall back to overall timeout during crawl/wait loops.
  - Added unit tests to verify timeout propagation in sync and async paths.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: abimaelmartell

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview Fixes timeout handling in the Python SDK by introducing a per-request request_timeout and propagating it through status checks and pagination in both sync and async paths. - Add request_timeout to get_crawl_status (sync/async) and pagination fetches; wire through client APIs. - Use request_timeout for each poll in wait loops; default to timeout when request_timeout is not provided. - Add unit tests to verify request_timeout is forwarded in top-level get_crawl_status calls (sync/async). ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

title: feat(nuq/concurrency-tracking): fix deadlock
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Fixes deadlocks in NuQ per-owner concurrency tracking by using deterministic per-owner advisory locks and smaller batches. This prevents stuck workers and skipped jobs, and smooths prefetching.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Acquire pg_advisory_xact_lock per owner in a deterministic order to avoid deadlocks between workers.
  - Upsert now increments current_concurrency and updates max_concurrency for owners.
  - Reduce batch size from 500 to 100 to lower lock contention; increase prefetch cadence from 250ms to 100ms.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(nuq): concurrency tracking
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Adds per-owner concurrency tracking to NuQ and enables it for the scrape queue. Job activation, completion, and failure now atomically update per-owner concurrency counts to reduce race conditions.

- **New Features**
  - Added queue option concurrencyLimit: "per-owner".
  - Prefetch and single-job fetch use CTEs to select and activate jobs in one query; when enabled, they increment owner current_concurrency.
  - Completion and failure paths decrement owner current_concurrency with GREATEST(0, ...); notification behavior unchanged.
  - New table: nuq.queue_scrape_owner_concurrency (id, current_concurrency, max_concurrency); inserts default max_concurrency to 100.
  - Enabled per-owner concurrency tracking for nuq.queue_scrape.

- **Migration**
  - Apply apps/nuq-postgres/nuq.sql to create nuq.queue_scrape_owner_concurrency. No data backfill needed.

title: Fix search country parameter to default to undefined when location is set
body:
# Fix search country parameter to default to undefined when location is set

## Summary
Modified the search request schemas in both v1 and v2 APIs to automatically set the `country` parameter to `undefined` when the `location` parameter is provided. This ensures that geolocation-based searches don't conflict with country-based filtering.

**Changes:**
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v1/types.ts`: Added transform to set `country` to undefined when `location` is present
- `apps/api/src/controllers/v2/types.ts`: Added transform to set `country` to undefined when `location` is present, and propagated this to the web/news sources
- Added tests in both v1 and v2 search test files to verify searches work with the location parameter

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
- [ ] **Verify requirement interpretation**: Confirm that "location" is the correct field to check (not the deprecated "geolocation" field in scrapeOptions)
- [ ] **Test search functionality**: Manually test search with `location: "San Francisco"` to ensure results are returned correctly and the country parameter is indeed undefined
- [ ] **Check backwards compatibility**: Verify there are no existing use cases that rely on both `country` and `location` being set simultaneously
- [ ] **Run full test suite**: Execute the complete test suite to ensure no regressions, particularly in search-related tests
- [ ] **Verify downstream behavior**: Confirm that search backends (SearchAPI, SearXNG) handle undefined country correctly when location is provided

### Test Plan
1. Make a v1 search request with `location` set and verify results are returned
2. Make a v2 search request with `location` set and verify results are returned
3. Check the actual request sent to search backends to confirm `country` is undefined
4. Test edge cases: both parameters set, neither set, only country set

### Notes
- There's a minor formatting change in v2/types.ts (lines 1453-1461) that was auto-applied by prettier - this is just code formatting, not a logic change
- The added tests verify that search works with location, but don't explicitly assert that country is undefined - this transformation happens in the Zod schema layer
- Session requested by thomas@sideguide.dev (thomas@sideguide.dev)
- Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/ccd7a776e9974705976470df10d9fa1a
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Make location-based searches ignore the country filter by default. When location is set, country is automatically set to undefined in both v1 and v2, preventing conflicting filters.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - v1 and v2 schemas: transform country to undefined when location is provided.
  - v2: propagate the resolved country value to web and news sources.
  - Tests: added v1/v2 cases confirming searches return results with location set.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat: index precrawl
body:
New precrawl system + fixed harness dev mode issues
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Adds an index pre-crawl system that picks high-priority domains, allocates crawl budgets, and enqueues targeted crawls with a new isPreCrawl flag for correct indexing. Also improves the dev harness with reliable restarts and cleaner shutdowns.

- **New Features**
  - Adds a precrawl worker that selects domains via query_domain_priority and fetches top URLs per domain in small batches.
  - Distributes a total budget across domains/pages and submits kickoff crawl jobs to BullMQ (respects PRECRAWL_TEAM_ID; supports DRY_RUN and DOMAIN_ONLY_RUN).
  - Propagates isPreCrawl through scrape, sitemap, and index pipelines (including is_precrawl index field and OTEL attributes).
  - Skips frequency recording for precrawl and improves logging/metrics.
  - Admin endpoint now enqueues a precrawl job.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Dev harness restarts: abortable waitForPort, robust service termination (force-kill in dev after timeout), and safer SIGINT/SIGTERM handling.
  - Workers use structured logging, wait for running jobs, and shut down OTEL cleanly.
  - Sitemap kickoff receives isPreCrawl to avoid misclassification.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: chore(python-sdk): Bump version to 4.3.7 for poll_interval fix
body:
# chore(python-sdk): Bump version to 4.3.7 for poll_interval fix

## Summary
This PR bumps the Python SDK version from 4.3.6 to 4.3.7 to publish a new release that includes the `poll_interval` parameter fix for `AsyncWatcher`. 

The underlying bug fix was already merged in commit e661bd2b (PR #2155) which added support for the `poll_interval` parameter to `AsyncWatcher.__init__()`, but version 4.3.6 on PyPI was published before this fix was included.

**User reported issue**: `AsyncWatcher.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'poll_interval'`
**Root cause**: The `AsyncFirecrawlClient.watcher()` method was passing `poll_interval` to `AsyncWatcher`, but `AsyncWatcher.__init__()` didn't accept it.
**Fix**: Already implemented - `AsyncWatcher` now accepts and properly handles the `poll_interval` parameter.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
- [ ] **Verify end-to-end functionality**: Test that `AsyncWatcher` with `poll_interval` parameter works with real Firecrawl API calls (not just the unit test)
- [ ] **Confirm automatic publishing**: After merge, verify the GitHub workflow successfully publishes version 4.3.7 to PyPI 
- [ ] **Test user installation**: Verify users can `pip install firecrawl-py==4.3.7` and the poll_interval bug is resolved

### Notes
- Local testing confirmed `AsyncWatcher(client, job_id, poll_interval=5)` works without TypeError
- The publish workflow will automatically trigger when this PR is merged to main
- Version check script will detect the increment and publish to PyPI as both `firecrawl-py` and `firecrawl`

**Link to Devin run**: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/28f74846e02c4bd48ca3508b4991d6df  
**Requested by**: @Chadha93
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Bump Python SDK to 4.3.7 to release the AsyncWatcher poll_interval fix. Passing poll_interval now works without errors.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(api): add warnings for low results and robots.txt restrictions in map and crawl controllers
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Add helpful warnings to map and crawl endpoints and normalize crawl URLs for better guidance and reliability. Users will see suggestions when mapping deep paths and notices when robots.txt blocks pages; crawl URLs ending with /* are auto-trimmed with a warning.

- **New Features**
  - Map (v1/v2): If ≤1 link and the URL isn’t a base domain, return a warning suggesting the base domain (e.g., example.com).
  - Crawl (v2): Return a warning in crawl status when robots.txt blocks pages; blocked URLs are tracked in Redis.
  - Crawl (v2): Strip trailing /* from input URLs and include a warning in the response.
  - Types/Schemas: Added warning fields to MapResponse, CrawlResponse, CrawlStatusResponse; introduced BASE_URL_SCHEMA and CRAWL_URL transform to handle URL modifications.
  - Tests: Added coverage for base-domain detection and warnings across map and crawl controllers.

title: Fix a self-hosted docker-compose.yaml bug caused by a recent firecrawl change
body:
Add EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT to docker-compose environment

## The Original Error

The Firecrawl container was **failing to start** with this error:

```
Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use 0.0.0.0:3002
    at Server.setupListenHandle [as _listen2] (node:net:1940:16)
```

The **API service** was crashing because port 3002 was already occupied by another service inside the same container.

## Root Cause Analysis

After extracting and analyzing the compiled JavaScript from inside the container, I discovered a **port configuration mismatch**:

1. **The `extract-worker` service** (`/app/dist/src/services/extract-worker.js`) checks for the environment variable `EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT`:
   ```javascript
   const workerPort = process.env.EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT || process.env.PORT || 3005;
   ```

2. **The variable wasn't set**, so it fell back to the global `PORT` environment variable, which was set to `3002`

3. **Race condition**: The harness script spawns multiple services concurrently:
   - `extract-worker` started first and successfully bound to port 3002
   - Then the `api` service tried to start and also bind to port 3002
   - **CRASH**: `EADDRINUSE` error because the port was already taken

4. **Additional conflict discovered**: The `WORKER_PORT` was set to 3005, but the harness script was also trying to configure extract-worker to use port 3005 via a different variable name (`NUQ_WORKER_PORT`), which wasn't being read by the code.

## The Fix

Set **unique ports** for each service by adding the missing environment variable:

```yaml
EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT: ${EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT:-3004}
```

**Final port allocation**:
- API: `3002` (via `PORT`)
- Extract Worker: `3004` (via `EXTRACT_WORKER_PORT`)  
- Queue Worker: `3005` (via `WORKER_PORT`)
- NUQ Workers: `3006-3010` (via `NUQ_WORKER_PORT`)

This ensures each service inside the container binds to its own dedicated port without conflicts.
author: th3w1zard1

Create the file or learn how to get started.

title: Fix YouTube cache markdown bug
body:
# Fix YouTube cache markdown bug

## Summary
Fixes a caching bug where subsequent requests to YouTube URLs return empty markdown content instead of the expected content. 

**Root Cause**: The cache stores `postprocessorsUsed=["youtube"]` but not the generated markdown. On cache hits, `deriveMarkdownFromHTML` sees "youtube" in postprocessorsUsed and skips markdown generation entirely, resulting in empty markdown.

**Solution**: Remove the early return condition in `deriveMarkdownFromHTML`
**Link to Devin run**: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2ba157906f6f4c528add9a17bf94b07a  
**Requested by**: @micahstairs
author: devin-ai-integration

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 2 files

commits.message:
Fix YouTube cache markdown bug

- Modified deriveMarkdownFromHTML to only skip markdown generation if markdown already exists
- This fixes the bug where YouTube URLs with parsers=['pdf'] return empty markdown on cache hits
- Added E2E test to verify cache hits return non-empty markdown for YouTube URLs

Co-Authored-By: Micah Stairs <micah.stairs@gmail.com>

Remove YouTube early return block entirely

- Completely removed the YouTube postprocessor short circuit from deriveMarkdownFromHTML
- This allows markdown to always be generated from HTML on cache hits
- Removed the test case as requested

Co-Authored-By: Micah Stairs <micah.stairs@gmail.com>

title: Allow self-hosted webhook delivery to private IP addresses
body:
Updated webhook delivery and safeFetch logic to allow calls to private IP addresses when running in a self-hosted environment.

If the environment variable `SELF_HOSTED_WEBHOOK_URL` is defined, the private IP restriction will be bypassed

This makes it possible to run Firecrawl with local/self-hosted automation tools (e.g., n8n) behind reverse proxies


fixes #2222


    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Allows self-hosted deployments to call private IPs for webhooks and fetches when SELF_HOSTED_WEBHOOK_URL is set. Default behavior remains secure by blocking private IPs.

- **New Features**
  - Skip private IP checks in safeFetch and webhook delivery when SELF_HOSTED_WEBHOOK_URL is defined.
  - Enables integrations with local tools (e.g., n8n) behind reverse proxies.

- **Migration**
  - Set SELF_HOSTED_WEBHOOK_URL in self-hosted environments to permit private IP calls.
  - No changes needed for hosted/public setups; private IPs stay blocked.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix: skip summary generation when markdown is empty
body:
# fix: skip summary generation when markdown is empty

## Summary
This PR prevents unnecessary LLM API calls by adding an early return in the `performSummary` function when the markdown content is empty or contains only whitespace. The change:

- Adds a check after `trimToTokenLimit` to detect empty/whitespace-only content
- Returns early with a descriptive warning message when summary generation is skipped
- Preserves existing summary generation behavior for non-empty markdown
- Includes comprehensive unit and integration tests

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
- [ ] **Test existing summary generation still works** - Verify that URLs with actual content still generate summaries correctly (no regression)
- [ ] **Verify empty content detection** - Test with URLs that produce minimal/empty markdown to confirm summaries are skipped and warnings are added
- [ ] **Check warning message formatting** - Ensure warning messages are properly concatenated and don't create malformed text
- [ ] **Test integration test reliability** - The test uses httpbin.org/robots.txt which may need to be replaced if unreliable

### Recommended Test Plan
1. Test a normal URL (e.g., example.com) with `formats: ["markdown", "summary"]` - should still generate summary
2. Test the httpbin.org/robots.txt endpoint used in the integration test - should skip summary with warning
3. Test edge cases like URLs that return only whitespace or very minimal content

### Notes
- Requested by @micahstairs in Slack #ai-engineers channel
- Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/408c198975a0408f9c49ad657f1674ee
- Unit tests use `as any` casting to bypass TypeScript - this was necessary due to complex mock object requirements but should be monitored for type safety
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Skip summary generation when markdown is empty or whitespace to avoid unnecessary LLM calls and reduce cost. Adds a clear warning in the response without changing behavior for non-empty content.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Early return in performSummary after trimToTokenLimit when text is empty or whitespace-only; appends a concise warning to document.warning.
  - Added unit and integration tests covering empty and whitespace cases (including httpbin.org/robots.txt) to verify skipping and message formatting.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(nuq/rabbitmq): add prefetching jobs from psql to rabbitmq
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Adds a RabbitMQ-backed prefetch layer for NuQ. A new prefetch worker moves jobs from Postgres to a RabbitMQ prefetch queue, and workers consume from it for lower DB contention and faster job pickup. Supports ENG-3662 job distribution.

- New Features
  - Added nuq-prefetch-worker with /metrics and /health; loops prefetchJobs every 250ms.
  - Prefetches up to 500 queued jobs per batch, marks them active with a UUID lock, and publishes to queueName.prefetch (durable, TTL 30s, max 20k).
  - Workers now consume from the prefetch queue when NUQ_RABBITMQ_URL is set, falling back to Postgres if not. Uses job.lock for renew/finish/fail and avoids exponential backoff under RabbitMQ.
  - Sender/channel robustness: close handlers, renamed sendJobEnd, and prefetch(1) for listener channel.
  - Harness/scripts: added nuq-prefetch-worker scripts and lifecycle; auto-starts when NUQ_RABBITMQ_URL is present.
  - Tests updated to ignore new prefetch metrics logs.

- Migration
  - Set NUQ_RABBITMQ_URL to enable prefetching.
  - Run nuq-prefetch-worker (pnpm nuq-prefetch-worker:production). Default port 3011 (override with NUQ_PREFETCH_WORKER_PORT).
  - No schema changes; deploy alongside existing NuQ workers.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
author: mogery

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 7 files

title: fix: Use port from env.PORT for API
body:
This pull request updates the API harness to allow the port number to be configurable via an environment variable, instead of being hardcoded. This makes it easier to run the API on different ports and improves flexibility in various environments.

Configuration improvements:

* Introduced a new `PORT` constant in `apps/api/src/harness.ts` that reads the port number from the environment variable, defaulting to `"3002"` if not set.

Port usage updates:

* Updated all instances where the port was previously hardcoded (`3002`) to use the new `PORT` constant in log messages and when waiting for the API to be ready, both in development and production modes. title: feat(nuq): RabbitMQ support for job finish events and waiting
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Adds RabbitMQ-based job finish notifications and waiting to NuQ, with a per-job “listenable” flag and per-pod channels. This fulfills ENG-3630 by letting the API wait for job completion over RabbitMQ instead of Postgres.

- **New Features**
  - Uses RabbitMQ when NUQ_RABBITMQ_URL is set; falls back to Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY otherwise.
  - Per-pod listen channels via NUQ_POD_NAME; jobs store listen_channel_id in the DB.
  - addScrapeJob/addScrapeJobs now accept a listenable flag; controllers set it to true for jobs awaited via waitForJob.
  - Worker sends “completed”/“failed” events to RabbitMQ and consumes/acks them.

- **Migration**
  - Run the SQL change adding listen_channel_id to nuq.queue_scrape.
  - Set NUQ_RABBITMQ_URL to enable RabbitMQ; optionally set NUQ_POD_NAME for unique listen channels.
  - New deps: amqplib and @types/amqplib.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: Reduce playwright-service image size by 1 GB by installing only Chromium
body:
This PR introduces an easy win to reduce the Docker image size of `playwright-service` from 2.55 GB to 1.55 GB. 🎉🎉

By playing around with the code, I saw that the Dockerfile runs `RUN npx playwright install --with-deps`, which installs not only Chromium, but also Firefox and whatnot. But the code only uses Chromium, hardcoded.
So by changing it to `RUN npx playwright install chromium --with-deps` it now only installs Chromium. 


    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Reduce the playwright-service Docker image by ~1 GB by installing only Chromium. Image size drops from 2.55 GB to 1.55 GB and aligns with our Chromium-only usage.

- Switched from "npx playwright install --with-deps" to "npx playwright install chromium --with-deps" in the Dockerfile.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: fix: Add Zod validation to reject additionalProperties in schema parameters
body:
# fix: Add Zod validation to reject additionalProperties in schema parameters

## Summary

Fixes an issue where schema parameters containing `additionalProperties` would cause silent failures in OpenAI API calls instead of returning proper 400 validation errors to users.

**Changes made:**
- Added `validateSchemaForOpenAI()` helper function that recursively checks for `additionalProperties` in schema objects
- Applied this validation as Zod refinements to all schema parameters across v1 and v2 APIs:
  - **V1**: `extractOptions`, `extractOptionsWithAgent`, `changeTrackingOptions`, `extractV1Options` 
  - **V2**: `jsonFormatWithOptions`, `changeTrackingFormatWithOptions`, `extractOptions`
- Now returns 400 error with OpenAI-specific message instead of silent failures
- Added comprehensive test coverage for both APIs

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

**⚠️ 4 critical items to verify:**

- [ ] **Test actual API endpoints** - Send requests with `additionalProperties` in schemas to v1 extract, v1 scrape (extract format), v2 extract, v2 scrape (json format) and verify they return 400 errors with the expected message
- [ ] **Test valid schemas still work** - Verify that schemas without `additionalProperties` continue to work normally across all affected endpoints  
- [ ] **Verify OpenAI limitation claim** - Confirm that OpenAI actually rejects schemas with `additionalProperties` (the error message makes this claim)
- [ ] **Check for missed locations** - Search codebase for any other schema validation locations that might need the same treatment

### Notes

- Tests couldn't be run locally due to environment setup issues (missing Go dependencies), so end-to-end validation needs to be done manually
- This is potentially a breaking change for users who were unknowingly using `additionalProperties`, but those requests should have been failing silently before
- The recursive validation logic checks for `additionalProperties` at any nesting level in the schema object

**Link to Devin run:** https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d5c3f4da6c234b1296a1009859fe9e8d  
**Requested by:** rafael@sideguide.dev
    
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---

## Summary by cubic
Prevents silent OpenAI failures by rejecting schemas that include additionalProperties. Adds recursive Zod validation across v1 and v2 and returns 400 with a clear error message.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Added validateSchemaForOpenAI and Zod refinements on schema params (v1: extractOptions, extractOptionsWithAgent, changeTrackingOptions, extractV1Options; v2: jsonFormatWithOptions, changeTrackingFormatWithOptions, extractOptions).
  - Returns 400 with an OpenAI-specific error when additionalProperties is present at any depth.
  - Added tests for v1/v2 scrape and extract, covering nested and valid schemas.

- **Migration**
  - Remove additionalProperties from request schemas; these now return 400.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(api/otel): much improved tracing
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Adds end-to-end OpenTelemetry tracing across the scrape flow with span instrumentation and trace context propagation from HTTP request to queue to worker to engine. Improves visibility and debugging of scrape jobs.

- **New Features**
  - Added tracer utils: withSpan, setSpanAttributes, serializeTraceContext, withTraceContextAsync.
  - Instrumented controller, scraper engine loop, NuQ queue operations, and worker processing with spans.
  - Propagated trace context via ScrapeJobData.traceContext; workers restore context to link spans to the original request.
  - Added useful span attributes (job_id, team_id, mode, crawl_id, url) for quicker diagnosis.
  - Replaced timing logs in parts of NuQ with spans for consistent metrics.

- **Dependencies**
  - Added @opentelemetry/api ^1.9.0.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(api): add x402 search endpoint to /v2
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Adds a micropayment-protected POST /v2/x402/search endpoint using Coinbase X402, combining SERP results with optional sync/async scraping and returning full content or job IDs. Limits results per type to 10, tags categories, and tracks credits and jobs.

- **New Features**
  - New /v2/x402/search route behind x402 payment middleware.
  - Controller supports web, news, and images; adds category labels and enforces per-type limits.
  - Optional scraping:
    - Sync: returns scraped content merged into results.
    - Async: returns scrapeIds; each job bills on completion.
  - Filters blocked URLs, logs jobs, and rejects zero data retention teams.

- **Migration**
  - Configure env vars: X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS, X402_ENDPOINT_PRICE_USD, X402_NETWORK.
  - Requires x402-express and @coinbase/x402 to be available in the API runtime.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: (feat/big-query) Big Query
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary by cubic
Add BigQuery job logging to store normalized job metadata with auto dataset/table setup and a safer schema. Centralized job transformation and hooked BigQuery into the existing logging flow, reducing storage by excluding docs.

- **New Features**
  - Log jobs to BigQuery when BIGQUERY_DATASET_ID is set; uses GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID or GCS_CREDENTIALS.
  - Auto-create dataset/table with hourly partitioning on timestamp; schema excludes docs and uses FLOAT for numeric fields.
  - Insert with retries and validation; added a query helper.
  - Added @google-cloud/bigquery dependency.

- **Refactors**
  - Extracted job transform and logger context to lib/job-transform.ts and reused across DB, PostHog, and GCS.
  - Updated log_job to write to GCS and BigQuery and removed docs from PostHog properties.

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat(api/map): use avgrab resolve
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Integrates avgrab resolve into the map API to directly return post links when the URL matches avgrab-supported patterns, with billing/logging and safe fallback to the existing mapper.

- **New Features**
  - Short-circuits in `v2/map` when the URL matches avgrab’s `resolve_regex`; uses `resolveViaAvgrab`.
  - Fetches supported URL regex from `AVGRAB_SERVICE_URL` and caches it for 5 minutes.
  - Calls `/resolve` with `{ url, limit }`, maps posts to `MapDocument` shape.
  - Bills credits equal to the number of resolved links and logs the job before responding.
  - Falls back to the standard mapper on non-match or errors, with a warning log.

<sup>Written for commit 365acf13a1ce8711cce34f4e80fcec223af5ebe7. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->
